108039.title: [Linearisation] MBC transform dynamic stall states in rotating coordinate system to fixed coordinate system
108039.description: After discussing with experts in the field it is clear that it is necessary to do a MBC transform of the dynamic stall states from the rotating coordinate system to a fixed coordinate system to avoid cyclic variations. This is a prerequisite for doing the eigenvalue analysis correct in the stability calculations.   This should not have any effect on GUI / user actions or outputs etc. Just more correct results. Acceptance Criteria: Dynamic stall states transformed from a rotating coordinate system to a fixed coordinate system to improve the eigenvalue analysis in the stability calculations.

The other one is the storing of contributions, which is displacement, energy, phase and also the name of the contribution (state). 113319.title: [WasimLink][Outputs] Create new Wasim outputs with filtered kinematics
113319.description: WASIM ( TD1 ) :  Wasim will be able to adjust for some of the non-linearities in the coupled analysis ( phase adjustment and wave direction correction).  To do this WASIM needs to read the variables that are used in the Bladed BEM hydrodynamic calculation : WASIM will need to read a file with a constant extension and format :  Extension #83  SINGLE precision. Contents:  filtered surge , filtered sway  , filtered yaw  add sufix to variables : Wasim ( see below to avoid disambiguety)  Bladed will output these files every time the filtered AND outputs for wasim are required.  TD2:  To link with the other methods (TD2) they are using the results API to reach the variables. But there can be an dis-ambiguity if the variable  names are kept the same.  So we should rename slightly the variables in the file #83 with Wasim sufix.  ​ This is to avoid inconsistencies between Bladed and Wasim.   Below an example of  table on the contents of the file :  data to write in the file filtered phase adjustment Yaw correction Surge Sway Angle yes yes yes filtered filtered filtered yes no   yes N/A N/A N/A yes yes no filtered filtered InitialHydroOrigin(5) No yes yes N/A N/A N/A No no   yes InitialHydroOrigin(0) InitialHydroOrigin(1) raw No yes no N/A N/A N/A No No no InitialHydroOrigin(0) InitialHydroOrigin (1) InitialHydroOrigin(5) We are aiming to have the InitialHydroOrigin here ( it should be 0,0,0) , but so that later on , when this restriction is lifted we have some consistency in the outputs . So that the contents of the 83 file are the global position of the node , opposed to phase and angle shift.      Note that the hydro Data can actually be in another Node different from 0,0,0.  and have a "dummy" BEM body at 0,0,0 to get this output.  Errors :  the assumption is the user know that the node is at 0,0,0.  We should throw an error if the Wasim Link Kinematics node is further from a 1 centimeter from the origin.  If tower kinematics output node is not a BEM body node - throw error.   UPDATE DOCS:  NOTE THAT THE HYDRODYNAMIC BODY POINT in BLADED needs to be at 0,0,0 to match the Wasim output.  WARNING : users for WASIM LINK with nonlinear corrections have to use drift correction AND yaw correction.  Alignment  with the WASIM development ::  single precision - confirmed.   Example : Attached an example shared with the WASIM team.  Acceptance Criteria: new output file with the 3 variables  update link documentation The following message should be improved/updated in BladedBuilder.cpp:

94936.title: [NG] [UPG] Exit codes should reflect if any errors occurred during an upgrade
94936.description: To support better automation capability, user's script should be able to determine if an upgrade was successful, if there were any warnings that require manual inspection, or if there were any errors that mean the JSON file won't yield a successful calculation. Currently, bladed-upgrade.ps1 returns 0 even when warnings and errors are produced during the upgrade, which is not good enough for an automation-first product. This can now easily be addressed after recent improvements in the Upgrader code base. Acceptance Criteria If one or more errors or warnings occur during an upgrade, there should be a non-success exit code (> 0) to reflect this. 0: The upgrade was successful, with no warnings. - Pass 1: The upgrade was successful, but some warnings were produced that SHOULD be considered. - Pass 2: The upgrade produced an output file, but errors occurred that MUST be considered. - Pass 3: The upgrade was NOT performed; the inputs were invalid. - Pass 4: The upgrade was NOT performed; a critical error was raised. - Not testable* The console help information details the error codes - Pass The log should contain a summary at the end of the upgrade if there are any warnings or errors - Pass When there are any 'not implemented' models referenced by the input, the exit code of the upgrade reflects this; the upgrade report (log) will inform the user of the details. - Pass Implementation Revise the exit-code implementation, to count the number of errors and warnings from the context Add test coverage for different exit codes Update bladed-upgrade.ps1 to honour the same exit code strategy update the prj-to-xml build to check for >= 2 Update the win installer test suite to check for the relevant exit codes *4: the idea is that you can't create that - it's mapped to the "catch any unexpected error" handler, which ideally we will never see, as it means there's a bug

102275.title: [NG][UPG] More user friendly error reporting in logs
102275.description: Currently when the Upgrader encounters an anticipated error, there are many cases where it logs an error message, and includes a stack trace. It should not render a stack trace when the error is anticipated. Stack traces should only be shown when the program has truly crashed; i.e. the error is unexpected. Stack traces are ugly and confuse the user, it gives the impression the software has crashed, where as in these cases it has handled the situation gracefully. Acceptance Criteria All cases where exceptions are explicitly thrown in Converter functions by code defending against invalid input, yield error messages in the log, with no stack trace. The only error cases when stack traces are written to the log are for truly unexpected exceptions that are raised i.e. bugs in the upgrader, or unexpected cases in the input files that we haven't anticipated. Console output should be made terser, neater and more consistent with other bladed commands, so it's easier to read and understand for our users: 'INFO: ', 'ERROR:' prefixes used where appropriate non-essential messages downgraded to 'debug' level model conversion messages distinguished more from the other messages as they are particularly significant Implementation Replace all uses of `BladedUpgradeException` with `InvalidLegacyDataException` Rename `InvalidLegacyDataException` -> `ModelConversionErrorException` Put this exception in it's own file, in the `Context` folder Delete the following existing exceptions which are unused: RuntimeTypeNotFoundException PropertyNotFoundException InvalidPropertyTypeException CouldNotCountListException FortranKeywordNotFoundException ProjectApiGetProjectException BladedUpgradeException Adjust logging behaviour in xml-upgrader.exe and bladed-upgrader.ps1 to yield terser more consistent output

112300.description: The following schema changes are being made for NG 0.7: BladedAnalysis.ComponentDefinitions -> BladedAnalysis.Turbine.ComponentLibrary BladedAnalysis.Turbine.Assembly.<node>.ComponentReference -> BladedAnalysis.Turbine.Assembly.<node>.@Definition BladedAnalysis.Turbine.Assembly.<node>.@Definition value becomes just the name of the component in the library... e.g. "#/Turbine/ComponentLibrary/TranslationForFoundation" ->  " TranslationForFoundation" Acceptance Criteria Dtbladed is compatible with the final version of the 0.6.0 NG Schema, ready for the 0.7.0 NG product release Implementation From a dtbladed point of view....   1) The ' ComponentDefinitions' property, that is currently a member of the ' BladedAnalysis' class, is being moved to be a member of the ' Turbine' class.   2) The ' ComponentReference' property, that is a member of the ' AssemblyNode' class, is being renamed to ' Definition' .   3) Also, the value of this property currently always has a prefix of ' #/ComponentDefinitions/' - this prefix is being removed, so dtbladed code doesn't need to strip this off anymore.   The blue team will do the schema changes and Upgrader changes, and we can hand you a feature branch that is all ready to go to apply the dtbladed code changes. I can be available to provide any further guidance if needed. Hope this helps.

112345.title: [5.0][time domain] implement startup events in example controller
112345.description: Not sure how adding this new functionality interact with normal operation function in example controller insert some if statements around normal operation and check can apply startup/shutdown? Acceptance Criteria: startup event has following parameters: generator speed to bring generator online initial pitch rate (to move pitch angle from idling to fine) Logic Set demanded pitch rate and demanded pitch angle (action triggered by event at start time - example controller reads the GetRequestedControllerAction() to obtain this). monitor the measured the pitch angle and/or generator speed If the minimum pitch angle is reached we move to normal power production and set the VSPR state machine to torque regulation. If the generator speed exceeds the maximum generator speed then break out of start state and move to normal power production and set the VSPR state machine to pitch regulation. Acceptance Criteria: Set startup/normal stop  data in the controller JSON input parameters? Document how exactly a normal stop works. When does the generator get disconnected if at all. Likewise document how a startup calculation works. When does the generator connect Ensure have at least one system test model using startup. steady control parameters moved to additional parameters Acceptance Criteria: JSON schema for example controller needs updating. The user should implement events themselves.

However, a suitable warning is displayed. Have referred back to TR for advice. Retested in 0.7.0-PullRequest8880.1053.33 - Pass all registry keys are removed (from Computer\HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\DNV\Bladed\<version>) - Pass the app bin path is removed from the users PATH environment variable - Pass Upgrades are supported: When a subsequent patch version is installed by a user that already has a previous version for the same major-minor installed, the new version overwrites the previous version - Pass The registry keys are updated accordingly - Pass The disk contents are updated accordingly - Pass Side-by-side installs are supported: two or more pre-release versions can be installed by a user on the same machine, and can be successfully used. Multiple users logged in to the same machine: - Pass When two or more users, each of which has the same version of Bladed NG installed, are logged in to the same machine at the same time, the Bladed application remains operational for both users e.g. when user 1 logs out, then logs in again, while user 2 remains logged in. Existing Installer system tests are green - Pass Implementatio n Application is installed on a per-user basis; in a folder location specific to the user, and only accessible to the user. User PATH is updated accordingly Change default installation location to: C:\Users\<username>\AppData\Local\Programs\DNV\Bladed\<version> <version> is derived as: If no pre-release component: version = <major>.<minor> If pre-release component: version = <major>.<minor>.<patch>-<pre-release> dtbladed is no longer renamed , and not on the users path Set user registry key with version and installation location (HKEY_CURRENT_USER), so that in future we can programmatically query what versions of Bladed are installed, and where: Install the Workflow backend to start when user logs in. 112662.title: [NG] Default values ommitted from Upgrader output
112662.description: Default values for primitive properties are defined in the JSON Schema. Default values of primitive properties are no longer rendered out by the Python API, as of a recent change. That is, if user's code doesn't explicitly set a value on a property, it isn't rendered in the output. They are still rendered by the Upgrader. And they are hand-coded, duplicated in the dtbladed reading-in code. It would be a consistent approach if the Upgrader (i.e. the .NET Models API) didn't output default values in the JSON; this would yield slightly terser JSON. And if the C++ Models API automatically applied the default values set in the schema, when reading in the JSON; this would deliver a consistent approach to default values, equivalent to the previous state for demo 2, where the Python API would reliably provide the default values explicitly in the JSON. This would also ensure that the default values specified in the JSON Schema are reliably used by Dtbladed; the behaviour we are promising to the users is being delivered. Most of this work is already done: See the following branch in `BladedX.Models` that contains the work to update the C++ and .NET Models libraries: `feature/112662_default-values-cpp-dotnet` See the following branch in `Bladed` that contains the work to consume these new library versions: `ng/feature/112662_default-values-cpp-dotnet`.   NOTE!! These contain system test result differences in a couple of floating model tests, so will require Red team support to track down the cause of these differences. See related work item. Acceptance Criteria When a user upgrades a project, they are not shown default values in the JSON that are assumed by the calculation code anyway Implementation .NET Models API does NOT render the setting of default values (done in branch already) Upgrader updated to be compatible with this approach (done in branch already) Branches - Repos System Tests are shown to remain unaffected (not done)

113423.title: [NG] [0.7] [BLUE] Aesthetic schema changes for Assembly tree component references
113423.description: The following changes have been discussed for NG 0.7: BladedAnalysis.ComponentDefinitions -> BladedAnalysis.Turbine.ComponentLibrary BladedAnalysis.Turbine.Assembly.<node>.ComponentReference -> BladedAnalysis.Turbine.Assembly.<node>.@Definition The JSON Schema type of this property, is changed from 'AssemblyReference' (which is actually incorrect anyway), to a new string-based type; 'ComponentLibraryKey' BladedAnalysis.Turbine.Assembly.<node>.@Definition value becomes just the name of the component in the library... e.g. "#/Turbine/ComponentLibrary/TranslationForFoundation" ->  " TranslationForFoundation" This is because: The software doesn't interpret the full value dynamically to yield a value from anywhere in the inputs, so it's misleading and confusing to suggest that it does Its doesn't make sense for the software to be able to retrieve the value from anywhere other than the component definitions, so it's functionally redundant to have to include it Having a value starting with "#/...", according to the semantics of the $insert, implies that evaluating the path should start from the root of the current document, but the current document might not contain ComponentDefinitions at all (in a distributed scenario). So again, it's confusing for the reader. The @ prefix denotes when a property is: evaluated by our software on the fully resolved model,  whose value is coupled to some user-defined content in the model,  when the software evaluates this value, it will yield an object in the model. The doc string defines the exact type of the value (e.g. it could be a key to an entry in a specific library model, or a path to select an object in a specific subset of the resolved model) Acceptance Criteria The schema exhibits the proposed changes for: ComponentDefinitions AssemblyNode.ComponentReference Still to confirm: other instances of '@' prefixes The code generation copes with properties with the '@' prefix: Python Models API reads and writes  .NET Models API reads and writes C++ API reads The Upgrader is modified to accept the schema changes All affected tests are green Implementation This work is currently underway in ' release/0.6/fix/component-library-schema-changes' These changes should be cherry-picked to 'develop' when complete. Models Repo Component Definitions change in Models repo ComponentReference name and value change in Models repo Other property name changes in Models repo perform on release branch (0.6), cherry pick to develop Bladed Repo These changes should be cherry-picked to ng/main when complete. Upgrader changes in Bladed repo in line with schema changes (ComponentDefinitions & ComponentReference) Notify Red team that changes are ready. Perform on release branch first (0.7), then cherry pick to ng/main

114365.title: [CLI] Add 'convert' CLI command
114365.description: ## Acceptance Criteria - PRJ to JSON conversions are performed via a sub-command on the Bladed CLI - The existing command options are available on the new command - The existing help information is available on the new command - The command is only available on Windows; it is not a recognised command on Linux and is not rendered in the help ## New Command ``` > bladed convert-bladed4 -h Convert a Bladed 4 project ('.prj' or '.$PJ') to Bladed 5 JSON - Converts '.prj' and '.$PJ' files. - Converts only the active simulation (the selected simulation shown in the Bladed 4 UI). - Requires a compatible Bladed 4 installation on the same machine. - If not specified (with '--bladed4'), the latest compatible Bladed 4 installed on the machine will be detected. Usage: bladed convert-bladed4 <PRJ-FILE> [options] Arguments: <PRJ-FILE> The path to the Bladed 4 PRJ or $PJ file to be converted Options: -o, --output-dir <output-dir> The directory to which the Bladed 5 JSON file will be written. (Defaults to the directory of the input file). -n, --output-name <output-name> The file name of the converted JSON file. (Defaults to the base of the input file name.) -i, --ignore-version-restriction Allow the conversion to proceed even if the input file was written by an historic version of Bladed 4. -b4, --bladed4 <bladed4> The path to a compatible Bladed 4 installation used for the conversion. -f, --force Force the operation to overwrite the output file if it already exists. -?, -h, --help Show help and usage information Exit Codes: 0: The conversion was successful, with no warnings. 1: The conversion was successful, but warnings were produced that SHOULD be considered. 2: The conversion produced an output file, but errors occurred that MUST be considered. 3: The conversion was NOT performed; the inputs were invalid. 4: The conversion was NOT performed; a critical error was raised. ``` ## Implementation - Implement backend operation, using the JobService - Port the powershell logic into a new ConvertJobInstance implementation - This operation should return an error on Linux - Out of scope: - The existing `-Schema` command - The existing `-Bladed4Version` command - implement the CLI command, using the WatchJob pattern from the 'simulate' command - The command help intro currently shown, should be included in the new command help - The exit code summary currently shown in the command help, should be included in the help - The CLI command should not be registered on Linux - Add system test coverage with a mock powershell script, so we can test without Bladed 4 & upgrader

115191.title: [NG] CLI reports product version not CLI version
115191.description: Currently, the version reported by the CLI is the version of the CLI itself, not the windows installer (aka the 'product version). This is very misleading when working with development builds, as the pre-release identifiers often differ between the two, so the version reported in the CLI does not match the product version the user has installed. We should solve this so that the CLI reports the windows installer version as the product version, but can additionally report the CLI and Backend version via the 'info' command. ## Acceptance Criteria - Running `bladed -h` and `bladed --version` reports the windows installer version as the 'product version' - Running `bladed info` reports: - the windows installer version for the 'version' property - a new 'CLI version' to display the CLI version - Running `bladed info --verbose` reports: - the full original sem ver of the windows installer for the 'build version' property - a new 'Backend version' to display the backend version - End users cannot easily or accidentally modify the CLI to display a different version ## Implementation - Support a `.properties` file, that is a `key=value` text file, base-64 encoded (to prevent accidental or obvious tampering) ```powershell $inputString = "productVersion=1.2.3-rc.4`nproductDate=1/1/2025" $bytes = [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($InputString) $base64 = [System.Convert]::ToBase64String($bytes) # ------ $bytes = [System.Convert]::FromBase64String($base64) [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetString($bytes) ``` - workflow build script builds one - CLI and Backend projects have a default one - Workflow version code looks in the bin directory for it - throws if it doesn't exist - reads in the 'productVersion' value from it - Validates the product major.minor are equal to CLI major.minor - Validates if product version is a release version, or is a pre-release of 'rc', 'alpha', 'beta', then the CLI version must be the same category - productVersion is subject to existing pre-processing regarding dispaly versions vs build versions - Windows installer build script is adjusted to generate the `.properties` file and place a copy in CLI and backend bin directory - CLI reports product version as the version - `info` command is updated: - backend 'metadata' call now reports backend version _and_ product version (and build version) - `info` command output renders product version, CLI version and backend version - format adjusted to below:# ``` Version: 0.8.0-alpha.884.302 Build Version: 0.8.0-alpha.884.302+sha.ce9847a Logs: C:\Users\treree\AppData\Local\Programs\DNV\Bladed\0.8.0-alpha.884.515\logs Schema:  Compatible Versions: 0.7.0-alpha.505   Build Version: 0.7.0-alpha.505 Calculation Engine:   Version: 5.0.9-alpha.883.2264   Build Version: 5.0.9-alpha.883.2264 CLI: Version: 0.8.0-alpha.884.302 Build Version: 0.8.0-alpha.884.302+sha.ce9847a Backend:   Version: 0.8.0-alpha.884.302   Build Version: 0.8.0-alpha.884.302+sha.ce9847a ```

115971.title: [SCH] Easier UX for all discriminator 'type' properties
115971.description: An aesthetic change to the NG JSON schema for _5.0_, to achieve a slightly easier/simpler UX for manual authoring. The change is ultimately simple and superficial, but it is a breaking change: _All 'Type' selector properties, across all objects, to be called `$type`: ```json ... "SteadyCalculation": { "$type": "AerodynamicInformation", "HubWindSpeed": 12.0, ... "AerodynamicModel": { "$type": "BladeElementMomentum", "MomentumTheoryCorrections": { ... } ... "ShaftBrakes": [ {    "$type": "SimpleShaftBrake",    "MaximumTorque": 12000.0,    "RampTime": 1.0,    "BrakePosition": "HIGH_SPEED_SHAFT_GEARBOX_END" } ] ... "ComponentLibrary": {    "Blade": {      "$type": "Blade",      "Modelling": { ... } ...    "PitchSystem": {      "$type": "PitchSystem",      "PitchController": {        "Demand": {          "ResponseToPositionDemand": {            "$type": "SecondOrderPositionResponse",            "Frequency": 6.28318530717959,            "Damping": 0.8        },        "SafetySystem": {          "$type": "ConstantRate",          "Rate": 0.0,          "AlwaysUseBackupPower": false        }        ... ``` This has a number of benefits: * The 'type' properties are much easier to quickly identify across all objects, especially when names get very long * e.g. here it's quite an effort from an experienced eye to identity there is a type property available: * ![==image_0==.jpeg](https://dev.azure.com/dnvdsrenewables/e1cbe6cf-53a4-4b75-bf1d-b5dcbb8d461d/_apis/wit/attachments/e8ca4626-0a17-4273-b30d-2c3980e719bc?fileName===image_0==.jpeg) * auto-suggest ordering * `$type`is rendered near the top, always in the same place - remember that the 'type' property is the first property that must be specified for discriminated objects: * ![==image_1==.jpeg](https://dev.azure.com/dnvdsrenewables/e1cbe6cf-53a4-4b75-bf1d-b5dcbb8d461d/_apis/wit/attachments/0a83cce3-c2a7-4608-bb66-dec83ad32a5b?fileName===image_1==.jpeg) * The length for the auto-complete suggestions is much shorter, avoiding a lot of truncations with the default widget width: * ![==image_2==.jpeg](https://dev.azure.com/dnvdsrenewables/e1cbe6cf-53a4-4b75-bf1d-b5dcbb8d461d/_apis/wit/attachments/0d8afb52-37e7-42ac-95e6-35177d97db38?fileName===image_2==.jpeg) * vs: * ![==image_3==.jpeg](https://dev.azure.com/dnvdsrenewables/e1cbe6cf-53a4-4b75-bf1d-b5dcbb8d461d/_apis/wit/attachments/83531d3b-c2da-4348-b9ac-f7aa3cb78253?fileName===image_3==.jpeg) * naming convention is consistent with `$schema` and `$insert` * `$schema` and `$insert`both change the JSON schema applied to an object in a significant way, and in real time. The 'type' property also has a similar effect. So the ` This has a number of benefits: * The 'type' properties are much easier to quickly identify across all objects, especially when names get very long * e.g. here it's quite an effort from an experienced eye to identity there is a type property available: * ![==image_0==.jpeg](https://dev.azure.com/dnvdsrenewables/e1cbe6cf-53a4-4b75-bf1d-b5dcbb8d461d/_apis/wit/attachments/e8ca4626-0a17-4273-b30d-2c3980e719bc?fileName===image_0==.jpeg) * auto-suggest ordering * `$type`is rendered near the top, always in the same place - remember that the 'type' property is the first property that must be specified for discriminated objects: * ![==image_1==.jpeg](https://dev.azure.com/dnvdsrenewables/e1cbe6cf-53a4-4b75-bf1d-b5dcbb8d461d/_apis/wit/attachments/0a83cce3-c2a7-4608-bb66-dec83ad32a5b?fileName===image_1==.jpeg) * The length for the auto-complete suggestions is much shorter, avoiding a lot of truncations with the default widget width: * ![==image_2==.jpeg](https://dev.azure.com/dnvdsrenewables/e1cbe6cf-53a4-4b75-bf1d-b5dcbb8d461d/_apis/wit/attachments/0d8afb52-37e7-42ac-95e6-35177d97db38?fileName===image_2==.jpeg) * vs: * ![==image_3==.jpeg](https://dev.azure.com/dnvdsrenewables/e1cbe6cf-53a4-4b75-bf1d-b5dcbb8d461d/_apis/wit/attachments/83531d3b-c2da-4348-b9ac-f7aa3cb78253?fileName===image_3==.jpeg) * naming convention is consistent with `$schema` and `$insert` * `$schema` and `$insert`both change the JSON schema applied to an object in a significant way, and in real time. The 'type' property also has a similar effect. So the prefix is a consistent and clear indicator to the user that they are affecting the validation behaviour.

116311.title: Upgrader populates Controller 'ParametersAsJson' when appropriate
116311.description: At the moment, external controller parameters are always converted to a string in 'ParametersAsString', which for JSON documents is not a nice UX... The embarrsing bit is that our example controller has JSON parameters, and the converter outputs them as this ugly string. We should at least use the 'ParametersAsJson' property in our own software for our own controller. Acceptance Criteria When an external controller's parameters are a valid JSON document, then it is converted to the 'ParametersAsJson' property, otherwise use 'ParametersAsString' The same converter strategy is applied to the following model classes: ExternalController ExternalModuleComponent ExternalModule Calculations run the same regardless of the field used for controller parameters i.e. The system test results are unchanged

116547.title: [5][cnv] Improve warnings and info messages relating to converter
116547.description: From Sigurd's comments: Handled by the workflow team: " Item 3 Should it not be made clear that it needs to be 4.18 when upgrading. I.e. “ Bladed 4.18 to 5 project file converter. … Requires a compatible Bladed 4.18 installation on the same machine.”   And other references. We’ll have a 4.19 at some point, but ideally no more new Bladed 4.x versions after that. Item 4 Tried upgrading Bladed 4.14 demo_a and was expecting an informative error that it was not possible and I should upgrade it first to 4.18. It’s not particular clear what’s wrong or how to resolve it or if it even can be resolved? I tried using the -Bladed4Path, but still same issue.   I assume the fix would be to upgrade the 4.14 file to 4.18 locally and then do the upgrade, but that’s not clear for the user here. Item 5 Consider clarifying that   “No installed compatible Bladed 4 version found in xxx…”   to make it clear that there is an assumption about the location of the bladed installation (working directory I suppose). Item 6 Warning could be rephrased to mention Bladed 5 instead of NG: “WARN:       - [Analysis.Turbine.TurbineOperationalParameters] "Optimal Mode Maximum Speed" is not equal to "Demanded Generator Speed". In   NG   Bladed 5   these values are combined in the parameter "Generator Speed".” Item 7 Warning could be rephrased to guide the user a bit more in case they have a monopile tower, but are using a multi-member tower definition: “WARN: [Analysis.Turbine.Assembly] Multi-member towers are not supported. For monopile towers, use the axisymmetric tower model. The converted project includes only the rotor model.” Solution, improve message: “Conversion of multi-member tower definition to Bladed 5 JSON format not yet implemented. Multi-member towers are not supported in Bladed 5. The Bladed 5 converted project will only include the rotor model. If an axisymmetric tower definition is defined instead in 4.x then this can be converted to Bladed 5 JSON format.” Item 8 I’m wondering why this is only a warning and not an error. Does this mean that I can use this as a hack to get my offshore turbine to onshore? Or would I have to go back to 4.18, make it onshore and then convert again: “WARN:       [Analysis.Turbine] Offshore turbines are not currently supported; all offshore related parameters will not be converted.”   Solution, improve message : “Detected an axisymmetric tower definition with offshore settings defined. Offshore functionality is not yet implemented. The turbine will be converted but all offshore inputs such as mean water level and hydrodynamic coefficients will not be converted to JSON format.” Item 9 Could consider changing this warning to a note/info given it’s impossible to resolve for the user as far as I understand. My perspective is that warnings should be possible to resolve: Could also mention the Fortran key card specifically for clarity: CALCOPTS “WARN:       - [Analysis] Calculation Options panel configuration , CALCOPTS,   is not supported for conversion.”   Solution = change to note " Acceptance Criteria: Improve warning messages and notes.

116577.title: [5.0][schema] align damping definition between blade and tower
116577.description: This is based on Sigurd's feedback on the beta, as in the comments. Plan address this feedback by making the tower damping work like the blade damping.  Tower flexibility : The documentation emphasizes FEM, but the FE model isn’t implemented yet—only rigid and modal are available, as in 4.18 . Selecting the number of tower modes was confusing; I only realized it’s implicitly set by the number of damping ratios, which is not intuitive. A clearer flag like numberOfModes, similar to the blade, would help. If fewer damping ratios are given, frequency-proportional damping could apply by default. To do: Damping for tower: Schema re-design & implement (See attachments). Edit upgrade code from 4.x Re-upgrade all models (system tests, integration tests, examples) Update documentation Tower FE model: Mark tower FE model as "not implemented". Acceptance criteria Range check damping ratio value. Must be number greater or equal to 0.

117155.title: Test
117155.description: Current message reads: Simple tower cans do not support torsion without shear. The missing degree of freedom is added to the system. Explicit tower cans are able to model this behaviour. Please update to: The Bladed 4 tower model has torsion flexibility enabled but shear flexiblity disabled. Bladed 5 SimpleTowerCan does not support this combination, so shear and torsion will both be enabled for SimpleTowerCans.  ExplicitTowerCans can be used in Bladed 5 to model this behaviour. Please edit the Bladed 5 model manually.

93302.title: [4x][dtrain damper] Disabling continuous DTD option in the UI
93302.description: Context Bladed example controller now includes functionality to simulate a continuous time drivetrain damper (DTD). The user can simply tune a drive train  This functionality is available in the Bladed UI. The user would tune the damper by setting parameters in the UI. A continuous time drive train damper is not representative of what a wind turbine would use. It would use a discrete time DTD instead. In addition, these are typically implemented in the external controller as they need to be tuned for a turbine and it would make sense to have all tunable control parameters located in one place. It is expected that all turbine manufacturers do not use this feature and implement the functionality in the external controller. This is based on the opinion of many internal control design experts from Energy Systems who are adamant that you would not use this option for design of controllers for production models. To see the window in question, open demo_a in Bladed 4.17. Power train > Select Electrical tab > Transfer function button. What needs to happen? Disable the "Transfer function" window and "drive train damper feedback" checkbox. Acceptance Criteria: 4.18 only - Pass (changes have not been merged down)  User cannot select the drive train damper feedback option or access the drive train damper transfer function window. - Pass No undefined model issues expected because DTD feedback option is unselected by default and it is an optional setting. - Pass Upgrade behaviour. Feature is disabled and user should be warned (User needs to click "Ok" on message prompt ) and the xml contents of the DTD should be removed from the prj/$PJ file. - Pass Opening a project for the first time. By defaults values in table should be set to zero - data is stored in xml so can be just cleared out.  - Pass Should be no mention of "drive train damper" in output files (such as $VE and in written project report). - Pass Demo models need updating - eng task. Considerations Investigate whether the inputs can be edited via Project API. - Pass 1>------ Build started: Project: UsageExamples, Configuration: Debug|x86 ------ 1>C:\DNV\Bladed 4.18\Examples\Bladed API\Quick start\Csharp\UsageExamples\HelloWorldExample.cs(33,37,33,54): error CS1061: 'IElectricalModelFacade' does not contain a definition for 'DriveTrainDamping' and no accessible extension method 'DriveTrainDamping' accepting a first argument of type 'IElectricalModelFacade' could be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?) ========== Build: 0 succeeded, 1 failed, 0 up-to-date, 0 skipped ========== Downgrade. No action required (not defined in later version and so should not be defined in earlier version).  - Pass No time series results are generated directly from this feature so should not change outputs. - Pass Encryption. No issues expected. - Pass  backend calculation has not been disabled.

111601.title: [NG] [WF] CLI has a basic 'resolve' command
111601.description: The CLI has a 'resolve' command, that only parses the JSON and mirrors it to the output. This story is a means to breaking down the overall effort for delivering the work. Acceptance Criteria The following commands are supported: 'bladed resolve <file-path> --output <file-path>' 'bladed resolve <file-path>' (writes to std-out) 'bladed resolve --' (reads from std-in) If any JSON parsing errors occur, appropriate errors are shown to the user If the file doesn't exist, appropriate messages are shown to the user If the user does not have permission to open the file, appropriate messages are shown to the user If any errors occur, the exit code is greater than 1, otherwise 0 System tests are added to cover the command Runs on Windows and Linux successfully   Implementation This just reads the file in, parses the JSON, and writes out the document This does NOT support distributed inputs yet Logic is implemented in the Engine: Refactor the protobuf definitions Single 'WorkflowService' for now 'Ping' operation 'Metdata' operation 'ResolveFile' operation 'ResolveJson' operation Move 'models' back to the Server.Grpc project Workflow.Engine exposes a 'vanilla' C# interface - I think we'll find this is in fact the most suitable way to do it System tests support providing input files 111602.title: [NG] [WF] CLI 'resolve' command handles distributed inputs
111602.description: Acceptance Criteria If the model is distributed, then resolve distributed files When there are many levels of distribution, i.e. a file inserting a file inserting a file, all files are resolved correctly to yield a single document When a relative file path is given, it is interpreted relative to the file in which it is defined When a file doesn't exist, at any level of distribution, appropriate errors are shown to the user informing them of the location and value of the $insert, as well as the path that doesn't exist When a file is not valid JSON, at any level of distribution, appropriate errors are shown to the user  informing them of the location and value of the $insert, as well as the path that doesn't exist When a distributed document is combined with the referencing document, and does not yield valid JSON, appropriate errors are shown to the user Out-of-scope: Resolving path-inside-doc references (i.e. fragments) out-of-scope: if UNC file paths don't work by default, support is to be added in a later story out-of-scope: if long file paths don't work by default, support is to be added in a later story Implementation Implement visitor and resolver, see prototype for some ideas

111896.title: [NG] [WF] [SPIKE] Investigate Windows & Linux installation and hosting
111896.description: How to install, host, upgrade and uninstall the new workflow components needs to be investigated and de-risked; this would suit an investigation before the formal implementation. I am hoping that once we have the answers for Windows, Linux will follow something similar (I anticipate it will only be easier for Linux). Acceptance Criteria An understanding is gained of how the workflow CLI and service is to be installed, upgraded and uninstalled on Windows. An understanding is gained of how the workflow service is to be robustly hosted and managed on Windows Can we use unix domain sockets for the architecture? Can we use a client-service architecture? What constraints or impacts are there on the installation and hosting if we are to use this approach? Demonstrate that any capabilities required on installation, and desired installation state, can be achieved via the Windows installer Notes The investigation should cover the following points & ideas: Constraints The service must always run with the identity of the current user, so that it assumes the same privileges as the CLI process (ie. the user), across the machine and any corporate resources (e.g. file shares) The service socket file path cannot be longer than 108 chars! Different users could in theory be using Bladed on the same machine at the same time (they could both have a current session on the same Windows machine) Only a single instance of the service per-user can be running at the same time (a user-level socket path would enforce this anyway) Requirements The CLI could have the ability to reliably, robustly start the corresponding service instance (if the CLI was a per-user install). If the service is not running when a CLI request is made, the CLI could automatically attempt to start the service, wait for it to become available, then consume it immediately - all invisible to the user The application must be able to function when a machine starts up, but without a user session being started - e.g. a build server scenario when the server starts up and is sent jobs to process Can and how do we support side-by-side installs for internal development versions Can and how do we support side-by-side installs for public releases of the product (e.g 5.1 & 5.2) Can we/should we support side-by-side installs for bug fix releases (e.g. 5.1.1 & 5.1.2)?

112646.title: [NG] [WF] Defend against circular references when using the resolve command
112646.description: When I distribute data, I might be using files that I am not aware of the full reference chain. This might lead to establishing a circular infinite referencing between files. I need mechanism to stop the process and avoid memory collapse. A warning stating where the circularity is, should be issued so I know how to correct the problem. I might be able to reference several times the same file, without triggering a circular reference error. For instance I might associate the same aerofoil data several times, and this is not an error. The circularity needs assure that it identifies a chained loop and not a re-referencing for different purposes. Acceptance Criteria A circular reference that occurs between files is detected and reported in a clear manner to the user. - Pass A circular reference that occurs between fragments within the same file is detected and reported in a clear manner - Pass Error messages should state the files and insert sites that cause the circular reference, to allow the user to easily identify the problem: e.g. "A circular reference is caused by the $insert property in 'c.json' at 'one.two.$insert'; 'a.json' has already been included.

112946.title: [NG] [WF] Simulate command supports all cases for 'output dir' and 'run name'
112946.description: Acceptance Criteria A simulation can be invoked with a distributed input file, and the fully resolved input is written to the output directory When a simulation is invoked... if an output directory is specified on the command line, the resolved input file and result files are written to the output directory ( DONE ) if an output directory not specified on the command line, but is specified in the input file, then the resolved input file and result files are written to the output directory if an output directory is not provided on the command line or in the input file, then the resolved input file and result files are written to the working directory Whenever the resolved input file is to be written, the name of the file is... if a run-name has been specified via the command line, then the name is <run-name>_resolved.json if a run-name is not specified on the command line, but is specified in the input file, then the name is <FileStem>_resolved.json if a run-name is not provided on the command line or in the input file, then the name is the same as the 'output type' used by dtbladed. e.g. 'powprod_resolved.json' What about multiple runs to the same output directory with the same run name?  Only overwrite the output files ('resolved' file and the results files) when a '--force' flag is provided, otherwise reject This forces the user to choose a suitable resolution - specify a unique output directory or run-name The behaviour of the Simulate command is the same if the input file is distributed or not, with respect to the run name and output directory parameters The only difference being that a 'resolved' file is written to the output directory if the input is distributed Implementation Parse resolved JSON for OutputDirectory and/or FileStem, if not provided on the command line, and the input file is distributed Use output dir specified on cmd line, else use OutputDirectory from input JSON, else use the working directory Use run-name specified on cmd line, else use FileStemp from input JSON, else according to Dtbladed calculation aliases The resolved file path should be: `<output-dir>/<file-stem>_resolved.json` If resolved file path already exists, then refuse to start the simulation, and inform the user a '--force' flag is required Update system tests & unit tests

112948.title: [NG] [WF] Resolve command doesnt over-write output file by default

The resolve command yields a similar experience to the 'bladed-upgrader' utility in this case; if a file already exists, the user in informed, and no action is performed Implementation update 'resolve' command logic add system test

112950.title: [NG] [WF] Automatic socket path resolution in CLI and Backend
112950.description: Acceptance Criteria CLI and Backend binaries connect to each other successfully by default, without customizing configuration binary artifacts produced by the build can be used by team members easily the installers have less work to do to configure the components Avoids any confusion of incompatible versions of CLI and Backend connecting to each other by accident Establishes a strategy that the Python API can follow; no custom configuration will be needed by user. Implementation Workflow projects honour supplied version number verbatim   ( IncludeSourceRevisionInInformationalVersion   = `false` in directory.build.props) Add the following  to  directory.build.props , so that local builds give an appropriate version value by default: <VersionPrefix>0.1.0</VersionPrefix> <VersionSuffix>local.dev+0000</VersionSuffix> appSettings.json in Git set to an empty 'socket path' value Change new Win Installer to render relative socket paths in the config CLI and Backend assume a default value if socket path is empty Windows : socket path in users home directory ( $USERPROFILE/AppData/Local/DNV/Bladed/<version>/.sk ) Socket path has a maximum length of 108 chars. maximum length of a Windows user name is 20 chars This leaves 44 chars available for the pre-release tag, which should be plenty. If it exceeds this, then just truncating it will be fine. Linux : socket path in users home directory ( $HOME/.bladed/<version>/.sk ) <version> is automatically derived by client and server: Inspect product version from assembly metadata If no pre-release component: version = <major>.<minor> If pre-release component: version = <major>.<minor>.<patch>-<pre-release> Grpc service should test for existence of socket file on startup, if it already exists, it should try and delete  it. If it can't, then it fails to start. This should prevent failure to start when a client has tried to connect before the service is started; in this case, the client creates the socket file, which then prevents the server from initializing a socket, causing a startup error currently for the server. Grpc Client should test for existence of socket path, and if it doesn't exist, it should not attempt to connect (and so prevent the creation of a corrupted socket file) System Tests should continue to override the socket path via environment variables.

113035.title: [Win64] Improve error message about 32 & 64 bit processes
113035.description: Bladed has the following .dll interfaces where we need to ensure error messages are informative when 32-bit .dlls are being called from a 64-bit executable and vice versa: • ExternalLoads.dll • ExternalController.dll • PitchActuator.dll • Gearbox.dll • Generator.dll • Wind.dll An example is given below for the external controller.  *** ERROR: Windows Error 193: either the dll is not a valid Win32 application, or a 32-bit process is trying to call a 64-bit process when calling 'Discon.dll'. Acceptance Criteria: Error messages are informative and clear when the dtbladed.exe calls the above listed .dlls in a different bitness.

Required argument missing for command: 'simulate'. While correct, it would be nice to present the user with a bit more info.  Perhaps, just a hint to run 'Bladed simulate -h' to see help will be enough here.

113145.title: [NG] [WF] 'simulate' stdout timestep display
113145.description: While testing the simulate command it became apparent that there is a difference in console output formats when running dtbladed.exe and 'Bladed simulate' Inputs: Any Power production simulation will do.  I think for the screenshot below I used the following demo project: Samples\PowerProduction\WindConstant\IEA_15_OnshoreTower_Powprod_WindConstant.json Dtlbladed.exe: The stdouts for initial conditions and warning message are output line by line.  One the dynamic part of the simulation station the timestep info in displayed on the same line. Bladed simulate The stdouts for initial conditions and warning message are output line by line.  After that each time step output is written to a newline Acceptance Criteria: When invoking the 'simulate' command with the specified input file, the output to the console matches that of running dtbladed directly; when the time steps are output, each new time step replaces the previous time step line that was output. Implementation I'm hoping that switching to an alternative event handler on the Process object, to receive stdout and stderror as a byte stream rather than as strings, will allow the control characters to be propagated through to the CLIs terminal, and so yield the same UX as running the process directly. using (Stream outputStream = process.StandardOutput.BaseStream)         {             byte[] buffer = new byte[1024];             int bytesRead;             while ((bytesRead = outputStream.Read(buffer, 0, buffer.Length)) > 0)             {                 // Process the bytes read from the stream                 Console.WriteLine($"Read {bytesRead} bytes");             }         }   Other references: Solving Problems of Monitoring Standard Output and Error Streams of a Running Process- CodeProject Interacting with Shell Applications (Made Easy)- CodeProject

113166.title: [NG] [WF] CLI can provide schema version info to user
113166.description: Dtbladed currently provides a `--schema` command, that prints out the version of the schema it supports... .\dtbladed.exe --schema built-with=0.4.0-alpha.9064 compatible-with=0.4.* The CLI should provide something similar. Acceptance Criteria End users can easily find out what versions of the Bladed schema the installed software is compatible with The version presented to the user from a promoted artificat, is always a release version (i.e. 'major.minor.patch') Internal users can easily find out what version of the Bladed schema a pre-release version of the software is compatible with Implementation The schema version info can be included in the 'info' command: > bladed info Version: 0.7.0 Schema: Compatible Versions: 0.5.* Simulation Engine: Version: 5.0.5 > bladed info --detailed Version: 0.7.0 Build Version: 0.7.0-rc.6.22+b.22.sha.abc123 Schema:   Compatible Versions: 0.5.* Build Version: 0.5.0-rc.27+b.13.sha.abc123 Simulation Engine:   Version: 5.0.5 Build Version: 5.0.5-rc.25.1658+sha.04a39f7 If the schema version is a pre-release, and starts with `rc.`, then the 'Compatible Version' value gets rendered at '<major>.<minor>.*' If the schema version is a pre-release, but doesn't start with 'rc.', then the 'Compatible Version' value is '<major>.<minor>.<patch>-<prerelease-tag>' The schema version can be determined in the code by referencing the BladedX-Models-Version property via the csproj: <Import Project="../../Config/BladedX.Versions.props" /> <ItemGroup>   <AssemblyAttribute Include="System.Reflection.AssemblyMetadataAttribute">     <_Parameter1>BladedX.SchemaVersion</_Parameter1>     <_Parameter2>$(BladedX-Models-Version)</_Parameter2>   </AssemblyAttribute> </ItemGroup> var assembly = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly(); var attribute = assembly.GetCustomAttribute<AssemblyMetadataAttribute>(" BladedX.SchemaVersion "); string myPropertyValue = attribute?.Value ?? throw Exception();

113250.title: [NG] [WF] Reliable workflow system test suite
113250.description: We currently have a few flaky tests in the Workflow system test suite. This flakiness must be resolved before the Workflow dev branch can be merged to ng/main and a release branch. Examples of failures: Workflow (Windows) / #190 | 0.7.0-wf-main.14.190 on 18 Mar 15:21 — TeamCity There are a few more examples in the build history, that are essentially the same cause. These failures can also occassionally be observed locally. Acceptance Criteria Workflow test suite is reliable Implementation I have a hunch that this could be caused by the use of ConcurrentDictionary.GetOrAdd(key, Fun<Value>); The documentation states that the function parameter is not invoked inside a lock, but the code is written assuming that it is! All these operations are atomic and are thread-safe with regards to all other operations on the   ConcurrentDictionary<TKey,TValue>   class. The only exceptions are the methods that accept a delegate, that is,   AddOrUpdate   and   GetOrAdd . For modifications and write operations to the dictionary,   ConcurrentDictionary<TKey,TValue>   uses fine-grained locking to ensure thread safety. (Read operations on the dictionary are performed in a lock-free manner.) However, delegates for these methods are called outside the locks to avoid the problems that can arise from executing unknown code under a lock. Therefore, the code executed by these delegates is not subject to the atomicity of the operation. See : ConcurrentDictionary<TKey,TValue> Class (System.Collections.Concurrent) | Microsoft Learn We should switch to using something like the following, to achieve safe adding/retrieval from the cache: var  semaphore =   new   SemaphoreSlim ( 1 ); // --------------- if   ( cache . TryGetValue ( p ,   out   var  val )) return   val ; semaphore . Wait (); try { if   ( cache . TryGetValue ( p ,   out   val ))     return   val ; val   =   Task . Run (()   =>  /* Yield value */ );       cache . TryAdd ( p ,   val );       return   val ; } finally { semaphore . Release (); }

As we expect our users will typically output to a file most of the time, and only output to stdout in rarer scripting use cases, the suggestion is to make the file output the default. i.e. Usage:   bladed resolve <INPUT-PATH> <OUTPUT-PATH> [options] Arguments: <INPUT-PATH>  The path of a Bladed JSON input file. Use '-' to signal reading from standard input. <OUTPUT-PATH>  The path of the file to contain the resolved JSON document. Use '-' to signal writing to standard out. e.g. bladed resolve input.json resolved.json To write to std out: bladed resolve input.json - And hence to read & write to stdout/stdin: cat input.json | bladed resolve - - | Set-Content resolved.json Acceptance Criteria:

113649.title: [NG] [RMS] Implement license checking in Bladed 5.0 workflow services
113649.description: Acceptance Criteria: Bladed workflow components are to be controlled through a seat counted license feature called BladedServices (Could change with token based licensing, but this is the current implementation) Starting the Bladed backend service in itself does not consume any licenses, licenses are only required to run the following commands presently: resolve - Pass simulate - Pass validate (to be done when ready, there is a WI to track it as part of "validate" implementation) - Not supported Note that the status and info commands do not lead to a license check - Pass A "seat" refers to a unique combination of Hostname + User ID This means:  if you run as two different users on two different computers, together they will consume 2 seats - Pass if you run multiple instances of the process on the same computer as the same user, they will together only consume 1 seat - Pass if you have two logins to the same computer and run the process as the different users, then together they will consume 2 seats - Pass Users should be prevented from using licensed services when the license feature is expired or license feature is not present - Pass Users should be prevented from running more Bladed services than their seat limit allows - Pass Bladed service versions have a built in build date, which is validated against the SLA expiry date on the license feature it logs into. If the SLA date is greater than service build date then service is allowed to execute, if not, command should return a suitable failure message in its response - Pass When a license session is established (due to customer invoking an operation) and the invoked operation has finished, but no further action happens for a period of 1 hour, then the license should be logged out of (i.e. seat hogging does not occur from backend services that are merely idling) - Pass If a new operation starts within the 1 hours period, the log out doesn't happen. The logout will move to then after 1 hour of the newly started operation.  - Pass If multiple operations are started in parallel/run in an interleaved way, then the last operation to complete becomes the time from which we calculate the 1 hour for logging out - Pass If the service itself terminates normally or with an exception then the licenses held by it must be signed out of before process exits. A log entry must be produced to this effect. - Unable to test, see note below from Tom*  If the service terminates in a manner that cannot be handled (i.e. a mode of failure that cannot be "caught") then licenses are released automatically by the server on a pre-configured key lifetime expiring (which is 30 minutes) - Pass Impl. Note :   Each of the services implemented/provided by the backend must acquire an appropriate feature when accessed. Which feature is subject to commercial model, so it could be that they all just check for the same "Bladed Workflow" feature to start with, but. make sure that services can require different features if needed *  You'd expect to find this in the backend log file. However, there is no mechanism I know of that would make the  service end normally. So, not sure how this could be tested.   Also, on second thoughts this requirement could potentially lead to misuse, in that if you could cause the service to fail and if that then released the license it holds, you could then use that as a mechanism to cause an earlier than normal release so you can then let a colleague start using it sooner than they'd otherwise have been able. Anyway, this is all moot given as we currently can't trigger this.

113653.title: [NG] [EMS] Add license activation utility to Bladed 5 installer
113653.description: This item is purely about its inclusion in the installer and users' access to it. and ability to launch it.  The requirements on what you need to be able to do with it are covered in  WI 113654 . Acceptance Criteria NG Windows Installer installs the licence activation app - Pass A start menu shortcut exists which launches the licence activation app - Pass, see blow The name of this short cut is: "Bladed 5 licence activation (<version>)"  The installed licence activation app can be launched via this shortcut - Pass The app can be run by a non-admin user - Pass* The activatation app is uninstalled when the version of Bladed NG is uninstalled - Pass *I have now spoken with TR and clarified the reason I was seeing the behavior I have commented on below is expected and is a result of the recent change to the Bladed 5 installer where it now forces admin rights to install, which it previously did not. This means that the License tool is installed in the Admin directory along with Bladed 5 itself.  This will most likely change but not until after the beta release.  **The license tool currently cannot be fully tested as the development work has not been completed yet and as such is outside of the scope of this work story. Implementation    Activation utility is a shared component produced for DS wide consumption, so we do not want to test every function point within that, but we do at the same time want to test the basics in the Bladed context (covered in 113654) It is a .NET 8 tool, so you'd need to include the .NET 8 runtime in installer if not already included and install it a .NET 8 runtime is not already present This "activation tool" is not to be confused with the "nlsutil" a command line tool used for activating licenses on the network license server itself. This tool is user facing and will allow the user to point their machine at the network license server (much like how e.g. users currently use   http://localhost:1947  to configure network license access in Bladed 4.x)

113654.title: [NG] [EMS] Users are able to use the activation utility to see and manage licenses for Bladed 5
113654.description: Acceptance Criteria When a user installs NG on a clean client machine, they can immediately launch the 'Bladed License Activation' app from the start menu and configure the network license server host name, which successfully initialises the license on the machine; the UI immediately displays the Bladed licenses available to the user - Fail the user is presented with the following: They are not able to enter the license server details unless they click on one of the language buttons in the top right, they then see this:  After installation, and license initialisation, the user can successfully use the 'bladed' CLI to invoke the 'resolve' and 'simulate' commands. - Fail - you need to restart the machine before a simulation will run, initially you see the below: When a user installs NG on a clean client machine, if they immediately use the 'bladed' CLI to invoke the 'resolve' or 'simulate' commands, an error message is shown, informing them that the license is not activated  the user can then initialise the license via the activation app, and then successfully use the 'resolve' and 'simulate' commands on the CLI immediately after  Implementation Fix the license XML file that is installed: - DONE Change the initial value of 'LicenseMode' to 'Unset', it is currently 'Network', which breaks the Activation app on first launch Remove the value of 'LocalHost' from 'LicenseNetworkServer' - leave this node empty 'localhost' will never be suitable for an end user - DONE Consider moving the 'BladedX / Licenses' folder under 'BladedX / Installers / Licenses   ' (DONE) ---- Users are able to launch the activation utility from shortcut in Windows Upon first launch user gets option to either activate against a network license server or activate from a license key TODO : Settings page and language page and what it does Bladed MVP is based on the network server. To activate select the network server option and press Next Enter hostname of a license server at this point (e.g. BRVD400144) and press activate. You should now get a success screen. NOTE : The image below is for illustration. The exact list of products could be different. Also, the UI may also be modified in future so it could look different. You can use the Reset button (at bottom right) to go back to the initial state which then allows user to enter a different network server/key when you want to change.

Standard input, no distribution Failures: '{}' | bladed resolve - - returns a failure,  warning message stating that the $schema value is required warning message stating that the BladedAnalysis schema has been assumed validation messages for the input document against the BladedAnalysis schema A non-zero exit code is returned '{ "$schema": "" }' | bladed resolve - - returns a failure, with the error message stating that the $schema value is required A non-zero exit code is returned '{ "$schema": "https://bladed.com/0.7.0/invalid/path.json" }' | bladed resolve - - returns a failure, with the error message stating that the schema path is not valid A non-zero exit code is returned '{ "$schema": "https://bladed.com/0.7.0/BladedAnalysis.json" }' | bladed resolve - - returns a failure, with the schema validation error messages A non-zero exit code is returned Success: '{}' | bladed resolve - - -s completes successfully A zero exit code is returned '{ "$schema": "" }' | bladed resolve - - -s completes successfully A zero exit code is returned '{ "$schema": "https://bladed.com/0.8.0/invalid/path.json" }' | bladed resolve - - -s completes successfully A zero exit code is returned '{ "$schema": "https://bladed.com/0.6.0/BladedAnalysis.json" }' | bladed resolve - - warning message is produce stating that the versions are not aligned no validation errors are produced (assuming the input doc is a valid 0.6 JSON file that also happens to validate against 0.7 schema) the resolved document is output A zero exit code is returned B. File input, no distribution Repeat the above tests when providing an input file, with the following difference: '{}' | bladed resolve - - returns a failure,  warning message stating that the $schema value is required warning message stating that the BladedAnalysis schema has been assumed validation messages for the input document against the BladedAnalysis schema For each property that has a validation message, the file path of the input file is displayed C. File input, no distribution, file output Repeat the above tests when specifying an input file (that isn't distributed) and output file D. Distribution TODO Implementation WorkflowService has entry 'resolve()' function, which will call ResolveService and ValidationService validation is performed on the fully resolved document, if the resolve is successful Update resolver to add a '__uri' property into the document, to record the fully resolved path of the original JSON resource Evaluate the JsonPointer for each validation error, and find the closest '__uri' property in the ancestor chain, to determine the file Add a 'file path' property to the validation report entry message update ValidationService to find the file path value for each validation error

114108.title: [NG] CLI performs schema version checks before validation
114108.description: Before performing validation via the CLI, it should check that the schema version defined in the data file is compatible with the schema version of the application. This logic applies to all commands where validation is performed; 'simulate', 'resolve', 'validate'. This story only covers adding validation support to the 'simulate' command. The valid formats for user data file $schema values are: <scheme>://<host-name>/schema/<version>/<schema-path> <schema>://<host-name>/<version>/<schema-path> Acceptance Criteria When there is no '$schema' property on the input document for the 'simulate' command: a warning message is output informing the user that the property is missing, but it is expected a warning message is output informing the user that the software is assuming the input document is a BladedAnalysis object, and the schema version is the current version supported by the software schema validation on the input document against the current schema is performed the simulation is started if there are no validation errors When there is an invalid value for the "$schema" property on the input document for the 'simulate' command: an error message is output informing the user what the error is, and what format the value should be. Some invalid cases could be: an empty string no version in the path not a URL no path following the version When the application schema version is a release version, and a release version is provided in the $schema property: The schema version check should ignore the patch version. If the major versions are different, OR  If the major versions are equal, and the minor version of the data file is greater than the minor version of the app schema version THEN a warning message is output, informing users of potential incompatibilities schema validation on the input document against the current schema is performed the simulation is started if there are no validation errors If the major versions are equal, and the minor version of the data file is less than or equal, then the validation should proceed without any warnings Moved the above criteria to new WI under same parent :  Dev Story 114467 : [NG] CLI schema version checks - Release version When the backend schema version is a pre-release If the provided version is not an exact match, then a warning message is added validation is performed the simulation is started if there are no validation errors The schema version check is only performed against the root data file; validating schema versions of distributed files is out of scope for this story Implementation Implemented in ValidationService.cs

114110.title: [NG] [RMS] Installer doesn't require admin privileges on install
114110.description: The NG Windows Installer on the NewLicense branch has been updated to require admin privileges, and bundles the .NET 8 desktop runtime.  This is in conflict with the current state of the Workflow hosting approach, of a per-user strategy, requiring every user to install NG, and so every user to being an admin. This story is to revert the admin requirement, and for the beta release only, ship the C++ runtime, and the .NET 8 Desktop runtime as independent installers for download, that must be installed by an admin first. For the 5.0 release, we will the switch the Workflow hosting strategy to per-machine, and include the C++ runtime and .NET 8 Desktop runtime in the installer. Acceptance Criteria The NG Win installer does not require admin privileges to run - Pass If the client machine does not have C++ runtime 14.34 or higher installed, then the installer informs the user that Bladed 4.18 must be installed - Querying as actually prompts to install 4.17  TR advises this is correct, 4.18 will not be mandated until the x64 dev work is complete  - Pass If the client machine does not have .NET 8 Desktop runtime installed, the installer continues and succeeds quietly - Pass Once installation is complete, the license activation app can be used successfully to configure the license server name, and the 'bladed' command can be used successfully for the 'resolve' and 'simulate' commands. - Pass Implementation On the new license branch (ng/dev/newlicense/nl-main): remove the admin privilege requirement remove the .NET 8 desktop installation scripts etc depend on the new 'self-contained' version of the license activation app: DNV.One.Licensing.Thales.LicenseManager >= 1.0.10-ci.43 Package Details - Azure Artifacts

114242.title: [5.0][simulation time] Investigate simulation outputs written from time t=0s
114242.description: Change time-domain simulation behaviour  Specifying a lead in time of X seconds starts a simulation at t=-X seconds. The results are output from t=0s (the simulation time origin). Rationale behind this is so that events (shutdown, start) can be specified once and will always occur relative to the simulation time origin. Acceptance Criteria: Simulation outputs written from t=0s. Lead in time of X starts simulation at t=-X. Changes to outputs: time from start of simulation starts at at t= -X s. Affects "Summary information " and "Control variables". Turbulent wind file shift may also change results. Negative time causes the turbulent wind file to loop backwards in time. Will probably cause results differences for offshore cases -see related work item Report on results from testing. In particular, system test consistency with 4x branch. Notes for documentation: Diagram of time concepts: Simulation time (time and origin) Time history outputs (time and origin) Wind file time origin Make consistent reference to simulation time and time-history time. ____________________________________________________________ Problems with the calculation Internal wave calculation. 114267.title: [NG][CLI] Verbosity option for 'simulate' command
114267.description: ## Acceptance criteria - When invoking the `simulate` command, users can control the verbosity level across console, message file and supporting files with a single option - The help command clearly describes how to use this option, and what each verbosity level means - If the verbosity value is not valid in any way, appropriate, descriptive error messages are presented to the user ## Implementation - dtbladed already provides a 'verbosity' command line option; the CLI should just provide a mapping on to this: ```text                        |   Console   | Message File | Supporting Files                      --+-------------+--------------+-----------------                      0 |    SILENT   |    ERROR     |     BASIC                      1 |   WARNING   |    INFO      |     BASIC                      2 |    INFO     |    INFO      |  VERIFICATION                      3 | DIAGNOSTIC  |  DIAGNOSTIC  |   DIAGNOSTIC ``` - Adjust the dtbladed behaviour, so that 0-Console maps to `EXCEPTIONS`, instead of `SILENT`, as this is the more useful option for terminal usage - Adjust the help text so that `ERROR` -> `EXCEPTIONS` to match the value that is used in the schema - bladed CLI help text should look like the following: ```text Options: -o, --output-dir <output-dir> An output directory to write results and message files to. This will override the location specified in the input file. -n, --run-name <run-name> A name stem to use for all of the output files. This will override the name specified in the input file. -f, --force Force the operation to overwrite existing resolved files. -s, --skip-validation Skip schema validation -v, --verbosity <Error|Warning|Verification|Diagnostic> Set the verbosity across console, message file and supporting files outputs. Overrides any values provided in 'Settings.Logging' in the input document. | Console | Message File | Supporting Files -----------------+------------+--------------+----------------- (0) Error | EXCEPTIONS | EXCEPTIONS | BASIC (1) Warning | WARNINGS | INFO | BASIC (2) Verification | INFO | INFO | VERIFICATION (3) Diagnostic | DIAGNOSTIC | DIAGNOSTIC | DIAGNOSTIC --------------------------------------------------------------- -?, -h, --help Show help and usage information ``` - A valid string value or an integer can be specified for the verbosity

114398.title: [NG] [WF] CLI validation checks disabled by default and only report pass/fail
114398.description: It is not currently possible to provide user-appropriate validation errors based on JSON schema validation; the errors produced currently by spec-compliant validators , when genuine errors occur, include many false-positives that are impossible to reliably filter out from the genuine errors, without re-implementing schema validation. This has been confirmed as 'expected behaviour' by a JSON spec author :  Use of 'unevaluatedProperties' and sub-schemas yields false-positive errors when genuine errors occur · Issue #911 · json-everything/json-everything We need a different implementation approach to automated JSON validation in the future. For the beta release, we need an acceptable UX approach towards validation. Acceptance Criteria When there are validation errors, the error report only lists which files contain errors; it does not list the errors themselves (because it can't do this reliably). - Pass When there are errors, users are recommended to open the files in VS Code to resolve the specific errors. - Pass Undecided: Validation behaviour is disabled on all commands by default ('resolve' and 'simulate')? users can opt-in with a `--validate` switch Acceptance Criteria The following applies to the 'simulate', 'resolve' commands when there are any validation errors in the input file (or distributed files), then only a single error message is produced for each file that contains errors, instructing the user to open the files in VS Code - Pass The '$schema' warning messages are only produced when there are validation errors for a file, otherwise it's needless noise for the user - Pass When all individual files are valid in a distributed input, but the fully resolved file is invalid,  an additional error message is produced with this information, and the validation error is reported against the output file (not the input file) the output file contains "__uri" properties that record the insert sites, and the full path of the file that was inserted, to help the user debug the errors. When the resolve is successful, these properties are not present.  - Fail the __uri properties are output irrespective of if the resolve command is successful or not.  Implementation Modify the ValidationService to simplify the error messages in case of validation failure

114463.title: [NG] [RMS] Linux calculation test builds successfully aquire licenses
114463.description: Acceptance Criteria The following test builds have no failures caused by license aquisition in dtbladed: Linux integration tests Linux system tests Implementation The test and dev VMs can have fixed config files because the host machine from where the license will be accessed is guaranteed to have a fixed IP (unless we change virtualization technology, at which point many things will need to change beyond RMS licensing). So, the build and test VM images have been updated and rolled out to all relevant hosts. Implementation (suggested by Trevor based on work done on Windows, BUT NOT FOLLOWED) The Bladed license XML file on the Linux VM is configured with the correct values to allow dtbladed to aquire a license from the license server on the host machine See the below Powershell for snippets to perform the necessary work on a Windows host As these are Linux guests, the challenge is to choose the best/easiest way to customise the License XML file on the guest One option is to run the customisation script on the Windows host to setup an XML file (but not the 'programdata' XML file that host workloads use), this then is automatically available in the VM via the existing mount, then run an SSH script to copy the XML file into the expected folder in the Linux guest # Find the host IP address to use for the license server from within the VM     $hostIp = Get-NetIPConfiguration `         | Where-Object {             $_.IPv4DefaultGateway -eq $null -and $_.InterfaceDescription.Contains("VirtualBox")         } `         | Select-Object -First 1 -ExpandProperty IPv4Address `         | Select-Object -ExpandProperty IPAddress # Update the Licence XML file $configFilePath = Join-Path $configDir "LicenceManagerConfigurationBladed.xml" if (-Not (Test-Path $configFilePath)) {     Write-Error "License configuration file not found at path: $configFilePath"     exit 1 } # Read in XML, set license server, and save it back [xml]$conf = Get-Content $configFilePath $conf.LicenceManagerConfiguration.LicenseNetworkServer = $ServerName $conf.LicenceManagerConfiguration.LicenceMode = "Network" $conf.Save($configFilePath)
