115042.title: [5.0] [RMS] Implement token pool based licensing
115042.description: This parent item to encapsulate all the work required to implement, build, test and operationalize the new "token pool" based licensing model in Bladed 5.0. This item covers both the calculation end as well as the workflow end. High level requirement : Implement a token pool based licensing model for Bladed 5 so that users can flexibly utilize Bladed licenses and DNV can offer a much simplified license model. How "token pool" license model works : User gets given a network on-prem/lease entitlement which contains a certain quantity of the "token" feature (e.g. called BladedTokens). Users when running our applications (dtbladed simulations or the workflow services) will then consume a certain quantity of tokens from the pool available on the network license server. Note: Token based licensing can also work in standalone lease mode. We'll likely come to it later (but not in first commercial release of 5.0). This could be offered as a solution for customers who require commuter licenses. Each program is at liberty to decide how many tokens to checkout for given operation. This quantity will be based on the value represented by the features being exercised. E.g. if you are running a standard onshore loads sim, dtbladed may choose to only take out 1 token, whereas when you run a model linearization calculation (which is deemed a premium feature), it may check out 5 tokens. The programs then returns (by logging out of) the tokens it reserved at a time of its choosing. For dtbladed it would typically be when the calculation has completed (or an error is encountered and a managed termination happens). For workflow services this may well be when the service has been idle for a set amount of time (say 1 hour).  If the program crashes without freeing the tokens it reserved, the server will keep the reservations until the "key lifetime" has expired. This is set as 30 minutes for our products. After this period, the license manager will free such tokens and they become available for other program instances to check out. When there are not enough tokens left in the pool at the time a program tries to checkout/reserve fresh tokens, the program should fail to proceed and indicate a license failure. Note that a given program can attempt to reserve licenses more than once within its lifetime and each individual attempt is liable to fail. Due to the lack of delineated features, users no longer buy modules or high value features from us, instead they buy a certain capacity (i.e. pool size), which is entirely flexible. This is the value we deliver to the customer. And for us, we get a much simpler business model in return. Note : The "token pool" based model relies on turning off license sharing (which we currently allow). I.e. each instance of programs that consume licenses will be given fresh tokens for each license login call irrespective of if they were all started by the same user or on the same machine. I.e. you will need to use a separate "product" with a different model if you wish to retain the notion of seats.

115044.title: [5.0][RMS] Dtbladed: Implement token pool based licensing
115044.description: Change license consumption model in dtbladed. Remove existing feature based checks and replace with quantity based on checks as follows: Acceptance Criteria: Verify switch to new model Run dtbladed against an updated license server with only the token based product (and none of the current products) and confirm that it is successful. Token consumption rate: All calcs up to and including basic onshore loads simulations consume X tokens per process Simulations that perform offshore loads analysis consume Y tokens per process Model linearisation calculations consume Z tokens per process Parallelism: When launching multiple processes (on same machine or different machines), each process will consume number of tokens based on rule above. So, total usage on server should match sum total of values for individual calcs If user attempts to launch more processes than the token pool size allows for, the processes that are in excess should receive a license failure message indicating the reason Token return: When a calculation/simulation ends, the tokens reserved by the calculation are returned to the pool (verify with WlmAdmin tool) When calculation crashes then reserved tokens are held by the server for a further 30 minutes (this is the key lifetime value, could be changed, but 30 is the default for this)

115045.title: [5.0] [RMS] Workflow: Implement token pool based licensing
115045.description: Change license consumption model in Bladed workflow service. Remove existing feature check and replace with quantity based on checks as follows: Acceptance Criteria: Verify switch to new model Run Bladed backend on a machine configured to reach an updated license server with only the token based product (and none of the current products) and confirm that it is successful in servicing commands. - Pass Token consumption rate: Each of the following licensed commands newly reserve 10 licenses/tokens unless already reserved Simulate - Pass Validate (Not currently present for 5.0 beta) - N/A Resolve - Pass Hold behavior Once a reservation is  made, the reservation is held for an hour after the last command exits - Pass If a new command is run during this wait, then the timer is reset for after the newly issued command finishes - Pass Parallelism: Multiple machines running backend services add up to total usage correctly - Pass If user attempts to start more services than capacity allows for then user should be given an error message stating the reason - Pass Token return: Once the time period of an hour expires during which no new service commands were issued, the license tokens are released back to the pool (verify with WlmAdmin tool) - Pass If the backend service crashes in a period while it was holding tokens the server holds the tokens for up to 30 minutes more before it releases them - Pass

116150.title: [EMS] Install new licence manager config both locally in the bladed 5 install and globally. Preserving user settings in global config file
116150.description: The "LicenceManagerConfigurationBladed.xml" file upon first bladed 5 installation, should be written to global location & locally with bladed 5. Global location => C:\ProgramData\DNV\Thales\Licensing Locally with the bladed 5 => C:\Program Files\DNV\Bladed\0.9\backend The file installed globally will be written only on the first installation. The file installed locally will be always written on every installation. This file will be versioned. Any time there is a change in the config file like say a new feature name or introduction of a new product, the version will be incremented. This version incremented file will be written locally - always overwritten on each install.  The shared component will check for a new local config file version and load the local file and global file and merge the user settings and save it to global file location.  So user setting values  like the  ' LicenseNetworkServer ' and 'LicenceMode' in the xml file  will be preserved. We're actively preventing the overwrite of this file on installation. This was to prevent the value of the  ' LicenseNetworkServer ' and 'LicenceMode' in the xml file getting overwritten  on installation. User would be pointing to their network value. We want to overwrite the file while preserving the values for  ' LicenseNetworkServer ' and 'LicenceMode'. Implementation: To allow overwrite, just remove this flag 'onlyifdoesntexist' in the installer script. Acceptance Criteria: Get latest shared component. Increment version in licence manager config. Install  "LicenceManagerConfigurationBladed.xml" to local bladed 5 install with backend. "LicenceManagerConfigurationBladed.xml" should be overwritten on installation but the values of  ' LicenseNetworkServer ' and 'LicenceMode' must be preserved.

Run 'bladed COMMAND -h' for more information on a command. ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - Users can easily find a reference to the tutorials and documentation when using the CLI - Pass

In this case, the file stem of the input file is used as the output name. - Pass - A JSON file with no 'FileStem' property defined passes validation - Pass - When a 'FileStem' JSON value is provided, but no 'output name' CLI parameter, the JSON value is used - Pass - When a 'FileStem' JSON value is provided and an 'output name' CLI parameter, the parameter value is used - Pass ## Implementation - Schema: make the property optional in the schema - Pass - Workflow backend: remove the existing logic regarding composing the 'run name' parameter when no 'output name' is specified. - Pass

116604.title: [5.0] CLI provides a 'validate' command
116604.description: ## Acceptance Criteria - The following CLI command is implemented: - `bladed validate <INPUT> [--skip-resolve]` - Pass - The input document is not resolved before validation, if 'skip-resolve' flag is provided - Pass - Resolve and Validation errors are output in exactly the same way as the 'resolve' command - Pass - If a `-` is provided as the input, the command will read a JSON document from standard input (just like 'resolve') - Pass - If there are no validation errors, the following output is produced: `SUCCESS` - Pass ```text > bladed validate -h Validates a Bladed JSON document against the Bladed JSON schema specified by the document's $schema property. Usage: bladed validate <INPUT-PATH> [options] Arguments: <INPUT-PATH> The path of a Bladed JSON input file. Use '-' to signal reading from standard input. Options: -s, --skip-resolve Skip resolving any $insert references in the input document. [default: False] -?, -h, --help Show help and usage information ``` ## Implementation - CLI command - workflow tests - Installer system tests

116894.description: To help users for internal testing, and be clearer for end users, the 'info' command could render the schema URL that is valid for the current version of the software. ## Acceptance Criteria * The CLI 'info' command renders a valid $schema value for the BladedAnalysis schema compatible with the current version * Creating a JSON document in VS Code, and using this value in a `$schema` property will yield a successful JSON editing experience * When the schema version is a development version, i.e. a pre-release version that is not an 'rc', then the test host name should be used. If the schema version is a release candidate or release version, then the prod host name should be used. ## Implementation ```text > bladed info Name: Bladed Version: 5.0.0-alpha.123 Copyright: (c) DNV Services UK Limited 2026. All rights reserved. Website: https://mysoftware.dnv.com/knowledge-centre/bladed-5 Email: renewables.support@dnv.com Logs: C:\...\logs Schema: Compatible Versions: 5.0.0-alpha.17.1867 Example URL: https://bladednextgentest.z33.web.core.windows.net/download/public/bladed/5.0.0-alpha.17.1867/simulate/BladedAnalysis.json ... ```

I think when I started I got confused with a few of these options, "   Acceptance Criteria: grey out / delete in the UI  Update the documentation  Disable/unhook variable from code: Downgrade behavior -> always add that at: 0,0,0,0,0,0 Upgrade behavior -> need to warn the user if different from 0,0,0,0,0,0 that this will be set to  0,0,0,0,0,0 .  I think we should leave it in the code for now , but hard-coded it at 0,0,0,0,0,0 ( and not read it from the input file) we may want to bring this option in a more robust way later ( see for example distributed potential theory).  I think we should leave it in the code for now , but hardcoded it at 0,0,0,0,0,0 ( and not read it from the input file) Remove this function and all of its usages: ConvertHydrodynamicOriginOrientationToBryantAngles. 107729.title: [C++20] Replace boost::filesystem with std::filesystem
107729.description: This item depends on the sibling being done first:  Task 92754 : Code changes required to switch to C++17 language standard Boost was added because the C++ 11 standard doesn't have support for the filesystem namespace. Following the VS2017 upgrade we now have access to std::filesystem which we should use instead of Boost filesystem. Note:  std::filesystem is already in use in CalcResults (added because postprocessing was always 2017) - so in CalculationResults it is probably just a case of removing some #ifdef statements and Boost package refs.  Actions: Target later language standard (WI87764) Replace boost::filesystem with std::filesystem in source Remove boost_filesystem GHPackage references from all projects Remove boost GHPackage references from all projects that only use filesystem from Boost Delete PackageVersions\PackageSettings props files that reference std::filesystem Delete libboost_filesystem.a (External\Boost)

107809.title: [NG][DTB] Ensure that the "Global tower height" are robust
107809.description: At the moment, the PushTowerDataToFortran assumes there is up to 1 Translation object before it in the Assembly.  To make this robust, either: Maintain limitation, but throw a NotImplementedError if it is exceeded. Allow more flexibility, but still throw if exceeded (for example, allow any number of Rotations and Translations). Use AssembleTurbine (maybe in a limited way) to determine the 3D location of the bottom of the tower. It should be possible to eventually eliminate the s(1) array for monopile towers, so the quality of the intermediate solution should be proportional to this timescale. As part of this item, it will likely be necessary or advantageous to refactor  FindParentOfType: Consider reworking findComponentsOfType to search up the tree as well as down. Consider returning a vector of all instances (there may be more than one Translation, for instance). Consider using a manual GetParent iteration, so that NotImplementedErrors can be thrown if there are any components that aren't accounted for.

DtBladed NG (Windows) / #1014 | 5.0.0-alpha.669.1014 at 8 Jul 11:04 — TeamCity (dnvgl.com) or NG Installer 0.5.0-alpha.26 and onwards: Build / #167 | 0.5.0-alpha.26 at 8 Jul 11:12 — TeamCity (dnvgl.com) I am in the process of adding automated test coverage in the CLI system tests for this The following bladed.exe changes have been delivered: A new `--schema` CLI option, to inform the user which versions of the schema the calculation engine is compatible with A compatibility identifier in the splash screen when a calculation is invoked: An additional quality improvement, is that when you invoke the `bladed` CLI, with no options specified, it prints out the help information, and returns a 0 exit code. previously, it would attempt to run a calculation and immediately fail as no input file was specified.

108193.title: [Linearisation] Identify and present only relevant coupled modes
108193.description: In the Campbell diagram, we only want to see the coupled modes that are dominated by structural motion. The structural modes need to be identified and selected to be output in the Campbell diagram. (Not sure yet about what to do for linearisation). One possible solution is the method used in HAWC2: Section 2.3 of the HAWCStab2 manual talks about coupled mode identification. It sounds like they run a structural only analysis to identify the modes of interest to plot. Then then add in the aero states and find similar modes to the structural only modes. Thus aero only modes are excluded.    https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&source=web&rct=j&opi=89978449&url=https://www.hawcstab2.vindenergi.dtu.dk/-/media/subsites/hawcstab2/download/manual/manual_hawcstab2_v2-15.pdf&ved=2ahUKEwjwqfL9_t2FAxUeUkEAHSMlBi8QjBB6BAgSEAM&usg=AOvVaw3z5g5BXFjncfT8k0fc-1_K Acceptance Criteria: Only structural dominated modes are presented in the Campbell diagram.  This can be tested by considering the related bugs where e.g. aero states modes appear on the Campbell diagram. Use this work to improve the naming of the modes in the Campbell diagram.

108210.title: [CoupledModePlotter] Error when encountering non-existing instance of coupled mode at specific operating point
108210.description: The coupled mode plotter has primarily been tested for simple onshore monopile turbines. It is necessary to confirm the behavior is correct for other cases such as: Simple visual parametric study of the number of blade parts: 1, 5, 10, 15 Multi-member support structures such as jackets and tripods Floating turbines A coupled mode can be animated by doing the following: Install a fairly recent installer on Main (f.ex. 4.17.0-alpha.158.421).  Run a "Campbell Diagram" analysis on the model of interest. This will now generate a ".%150" file with the structural state positions and a ".vis" file. Build " CoupledModePlotter " project contained in "LinearModel.sln" to obtain the " CoupledModePlotter.exe ". Run " CoupledModePlotter.exe <path to run results from Campbell diagram analysis, f.ex. "path/lin1" >" and provide the folder where the  "Campbell Diagram" analysis outputs are located. This generates a folder for each coupled mode. ("C:\DNV\Bladed 4.17.0-alpha.158.421\results\lin1_stall\lin1_stall") Visualize a coupled mode by running "GH.Bladed.ModuleEditor.NewProcess.exe <path to .vis file in CoupledModePlotter outputs>" from the installation directory. Note:  The "CoupledModePlotter.exe" might be moved into the LinearModel.sln, check with Paul if issues with the procedure above. Support for animating coupled modes in results viewer is coming. Will also mentioned that the modal_matrix might have a bug where it does not change between operating points in the CoupledModePlotter code. Check and figure out if this is the case, if it can be explained or if it needs to be fixed.   Acceptance Criteria: Investigation into coupled mode animations for different turbine configurations, environment and w. different scaling has been concluded. Any additional work has been raised.

110169.title: [4x][gpu][vortex wake] read from configuration file
110169.description: User could have multiple graphics cards on their machine and want to select a specific graphics card for processing. U ser will want to run simulations on multiple computers. Need to be able to set the graphics card per computer. When dtbladed runs it needs to be able to detect what graphics to use on a particular machine. Assumptions - each instance of dtbladed running on the same computer will use the same graphics card on a computer for computation for now. From discussion with Tom and Robing (Søren): Default Behavior GPU Selection: Choose the GPU with the most cores. Output: Print the chosen GPU. Path: If no path is specified. Error Handling: If no GPU is found that supports OpenCL, display an error message. Non-Default Behavior Path to JSON: Specify a path to a JSON file containing the GPU name. GPU Name: Extract the GPU name from the JSON file. Fallback: If the GPU name is not found, display a warning and fall back to the default behavior. Don't Use Schema - Overkill Example JSON File: {   "gpu_selection": {     "device_name": "NVIDIA T600 Laptop GPU"   } } Acceptance Criteria: Use a configuration file setup on a computer that can be read by dtbladed at runtime in order to select a graphics card dtbladed searches for configuration file on both Linux/Windows: Implement a suitable search order: starting with next to the dtbladed application and then search directories -windows - store a configuration file in a subdirectory such as %appdata%. -linux - use the etc/dtbladedlinux directory to store a configuration file. Format of the configuration file (JSON most likely to be consistent with NG and Bladed example controller). Testing implemented on system test and performance test machines. Error if GPU calculation option is selected while configuration file not found (or print a message saying could not find graphics card specified in config file instead using default option), topic UX.  Add a Checkbox with "Use config" file? Configuration file needs to be versioned. For instance, in next major version of dtbladed we may want to add additional content to the JSON file.

110217.title: [BEMyaw] Implementation on how 2OH interpolation is dealt with
110217.description: We need a  new interpolation method for QTF.   At the moment the calculation of the QTF force is done at discrete time steps defined by the user.  This is ok when there is no yaw interpolation because  we can easily compute the 2OH force in advance. But when we need to update the direction of the vessel, then we need to include the vessel computation in the interpolation  After some discussions we have decided to go for a simple approach that reduces memory and required minimal changes. we can then check if performance is OK, and if there is no large discontinuities in the results.  Note that at the moment we are having memory limitations (that will be resolved with 64bit). Potential solution is for user to set up a threshold for QTF update (we will need to update the UI after if we see this is the way further).   Acceptance Criteria: QTF forces are computed based on the current yaw angle using two QTFs for adjacent angles.

110407.title: Disable end stops for linearisation
110407.description: Decided to automatically disable end stops for linearisation. Reason: Technically they could be activated, but i can't think of any use-case why you want to have them activated (see also guidance document in the parent). For active pitch actuators the multibody addon is only activated after initial conditions have been found (in status ALLOCATE_INITIALISE). That results in a large non-zero mean for the pitch acceleration if the pitch angle is near the end stop limit (the spring force of the end stop is added after the initial conditions). See also related bug #110398

110539.title: [Linearisation] Pitch drive states are not ignored in campbell

110628.title: [4.17] Enforce equilibrium based IFC method for blades to address wrong Mz
110628.description: The internal load Mz (torsional component) is erroneous when the <IgnoreSCAxisOrientationTrans>  is true  and the  <UseEquilibriumBasedIFCMethodForBlades>  is false . This is a limitation in the non-prismatic beam element that occurs for the conventional way of calculating internal forces when unique values are used for the shear centre at the two beam element ends.  The general direction of travel is to only use the equilibrium based IFC method for all flexible bodies and remove the conventional method. However, this requires significant effort, which is out of scope for the current work. The proposed solution to tackle this issue is to enforce the  equilibrium based IFC method for the blades. Acceptance Criteria: Bladed 4.17 and onwards will remove the  <UseEquilibriumBasedIFCMethodForBlades>   option from the data model and always enforce the equilbrium based IFC method on the blades. Note that a unit or integration test will be added to handle the case where the IFC method accidently gets switched back to the old one for the blades.

106951.title: [5.0][modal analysis] Run Modal Analysis as a standalone calculation with outputs
106951.description: https://dnv.dovetail.com/data/7tg5JDa7sA00ctyMbyFdF From user feedback both in support cases and also engagement in the NG project the mode name and frequency are important information from the user perspective to set damping for load simulation. An example workflow discussed with Enercon 17.12.2021 is to run modal analysis via the command line that outputs two .out files containing the names and frequencies of either the blade or tower modes. This data can then be inserted into e project file. This workflow was then communicated to GE in March 2024. In Feb 2024 a user at Ming Yang wanted to run modal analysis as part of batch however it was not supported because modal analysis writes information to the project file and therefore is not compatible with batch. It's possible that they could have used the workflow described to Enercon to solve the problem. Key requirements: Run modal analysis as standalone calculation Compute mode shapes of flexible bodies such as tower and blades Mode shape data to save: frequency, name, mode shape Assign damping values post modal analysis (user has to automate this part themselves by reading the data from the modal outputs and connect that information with their own analysis). Agree output format with customers Testing in place Secondary requirements / extenions: Supply demo model and tutorial - is this needed? Visualisation of mode shape (out of scope for Bladed 5.0?) Caching to save calculation time  (out of scope for Bladed 5.0?) Investigate: Run Modal Analysis as a service and return modes data User notes: "The frequency values are used for comparison with other files, e.g. to see the effect of changes in the $PJ file. Those changes are not necessarily done in the GUI but could also be done by opening a $PJ with a file editor. That is the reason for the requirement for the calculation of the frequencies without the GUI. If that is not possible we need to see whether we can find a set up with BladedAPI, admin rights and python 32bit." Following requests: 00124486 | Case | Salesforce  - Enercon - want to automate getting .out file mode shapes results back into input deck 00188840 | Case | Salesforce  - Thierry Maedar (GE) - wants to run from command line and access modal analysis results from .out file 00188458 | Case | Salesforce  - Yong Ren (MY) - wants to run from command line and in batch Shanghai Electric / Envision - Stated need for modal analysis calculation to set damping of mode shapes as part of Next Gen power production demo feedback.

108196.title: [Linearisation] Refinement of the perturbation values used for calculating the A matrix
108196.description: The current numerical calculation of the A matrix uses an absolute permutation magnitude for zero states and a relative value otherwise. The absolute permutation magnitude is based on the absolute tolerance also used by some explicit integrators. For most states (if not all) this absolute tolerance has a fixed hardcoded value of 1e-6. The use of a common absolute permutation magnitude for all states can be problematic because the ranges of the states generally vary with order of magnitude. Moreover, the use of a relative permutation matrix can also be problematic, for example in case the state value varies from a very small number to a large number. The aim of this task is : change the definition of permutation magnitudes in order to use state specific absolute values. It is supposed that the epsilon values used for the implicit Newmark-beta integrator can be used for generating such a set of absolute permutation magnitudes. Verify results convergence ( system tests  + full  22MW)  ( check if 22MW turbine is in the system tests).  Quantify performance impacts of the improvement .

110847.title: [4x][Linearisation] Naming changes for tower modes
110847.description: The updated naming convention for 4.17 doesn’t produce very nice tower mode names, I think this is going to attract support questions. (See results below from IEA 22).: Proposed solution :  changing to a set of ”natural” tower modes calculated as eigenmodes with fixed tower base and rigid RNA using the same method we are using for the blade Erik's email:  For the blades modelled by the multi-part blade model, we do the Campbell diagram analysis in states relating to full blade modes (rather than states relating to multi-part modes) using a simple linear transformation. I guess this is done to ensure ”natural” blade modes that would be expected by the user.   For the tower we do the Campbell diagram analysis in the original states relating to normal and attachment tower modes, which is the root cause of the naming issue. Perhaps we should consider changing to a set of ”natural” tower modes calculated as eigenmodes with fixed tower base and rigid RNA using the same method we are using for the blades. This change would be relavely straightforward because we can reuse the special class that implements the method for the blades. Acceptance Criteria: remove strange tower names from the Campbell diagram.

111419.title: [Win64] Port CoupledModePlotter to 64-bit
111419.description: Bladed 64bit can be used for linearization and it's expected to solve some known issues with the memory limitation in 32bit.    So it will be good to also enable the CoupleModePlotter for 64bit.  Acceptance Criteria: able to run couple mode plotter with bladed 64bit  check if  assertion failures mentioned below are a problem and if so identify cause.   make sure system tests 32 vs 64 are passing ( and enabled , if any disabled because of he existing limitation).  Some background:  From:  Martin, Paul <paul.martin@dnv.com> Sent:  Friday, November 8, 2024 2:07 PM To:  Oxley, Russell <russell.oxley@dnv.com>; Alexandre, Armando <Armando.Alexandre@dnv.com> Cc:  Jacob, Tom <tom.jacob@dnv.com> Subject:  RE: coupled mode plotter   Was this disabled in 64 bit because of a known problem or because its results hadn’t been tested? It doesn’t work in 64-bit – some runs terminate early due to an assert in the ghmatvec code.  The scope for the release was 32-bit only so we didn’t spend any time getting 64-bit to work.   As it is currently it not only does not error but it also still produces some other results files. The lack of error is intended.  The coupled mode plotter is an extension to a linearisation calculation.  The linearisation outputs are produced first and then used by the coupled mode plotter to generate coupled modes that may be visualised.  The results you’re seeing are probably the linearisation outputs.  If it errored, every 64-bit linearisation run would return an error including when the user is not interested in the coupled mode plotter outputs.   The missing outputs break an integration test in 64bit, and flag as a difference in the win32 vs win64 system test results comparison. The integration test should ignore 64-bit coupled mode plotter results, as they cannot be produced reliably yet.  The coupled mode plotter results are written to a clearly-named subfolder of the linearisation output folder, e.g.   lin1_CoupledModePlotterResults_ScalingFactor_1.0 . For the 32 vs 64, if there’s no easy way to compare just the linearisation results and ignore the coupled mode plotter results, perhaps remove all linearisation calculations from the comparison until the coupled mode plotter has been ported.   it also takes 18mins to do the comparison The coupled mode plotter produces a large number of files (typically thousands) for any linearisation run.   Thanks Paul   From:  Oxley, Russell <russell.oxley@dnv.com> Sent:  08 November 2024 12:19 To:  Alexandre, Armando <Armando.Alexandre@dnv.com>; Martin, Paul <paul.martin@dnv.com> Cc:  Jacob, Tom <tom.jacob@dnv.com> Subject:  RE: coupled mode plotter   It turns out that those results that are output by the 64bit version are different from the 32bit such that compare groups flags many differences – it also takes 18mins to do the comparison where some other comparisons typically take a few seconds     From:  Oxley, Russell Sent:  08 November 2024 11:37 To:  Alexandre, Armando < Armando.Alexandre@dnv.com >; Martin, Paul < paul.martin@dnv.com > Cc:  Jacob, Tom < tom.jacob@dnv.com > Subject:  coupled mode plotter   Hi   The coupled mode plotter code is disabled in 64bit and linux…   …   Although it is writing to stderr, it still returns 0 and the error is lost in the mix, in that dtbladed doesn’t return an error.   The missing outputs break an integration test in 64bit, and flag as a difference in the win32 vs win64 system test results comparison.   Was this disabled in 64 bit because of a known problem or because its results hadn’t been tested?   I think in Linux the test is currently skipped. If we don’t support it in 64bit (yet) then it can be skipped there too, but I think if we do that then it should report a clear error message. As it is currently it not only does not error but it also still produces some other results files. Maybe we are confident that the validity of the content of those files can be inferred from other tests (I don’t know), but otherwise, if we skip the test totally in win64 and linux then we have untested outputs.   If the chosen 64bit behaviour is to return an error, then we should test that (it might just mean adapting an existing test that checks the linux case)   These outputs also break some assumptions elsewhere that a single dtbladed run puts its results in a folder with a single TE file, instead for these runs we have subfolders each with their own TE. That might be fine, but it was unexpected.   Thanks

111937.title: Results API: Provide automatic variable name resolution
111937.description: Add a feature to the Results API to find and return a group or variable that was renamed in a previous Bladed release regardless whether the calling code passes the old or the new name.   The user doesn't need to do anything extra for this to happen. The lookup should work as follows: Try the name that was passed in (old/new). If the lookup failed, seek alternative name in internal lookup table (new/old) and if one was found, try lookup again with alternative name. If a variable with the alternative name is found, write a note to the Python console. If there is no match, raise exception as per existing behaviour. If a variable with alternative name was found, a console message should make it clear that the returned variable does not have the same name as the requested one but is equivalent (Python only): *** Note: Variable requested was 'Blade 1 Mx (Principal axes)';  found 'Blade 1 Mx (Principal elastic axes)' which is equivalent. This change applies to the following functions: run.get_variable_1d run.get_variable_2d run.unload_variable run.get_group group.get_variable_1d group.get_variable_2d The feature is enabled by default, and  can be disabled via settings as follows: ResultsApi.SearchSettings.retry_using_alternative_name = False If disabled , an exception is thrown when a name is passed that is not found (== previous behaviour).   T he exception message should list any "alternative" names the Results API knows about, if any: Calculation Results error: The variable was not found. Further information: Variable name: 'Blade 1 Mx (Principal axes)' Run path: C:\work\Repos\Bladed\Source\BranchedWolverine\Source\CalculationResults\GH.CalculationResultsIntegrationTests\TestData\Runs\Rename\Substring\powprod_new_name The requested variable was renamed in a previous release. Possible alternative names for the same variable include: Blade 1 Mx (Principal elastic axes) Test Pip installable package here:  http://builds.stp.dnvgl.com/repository/download/TurbineEng_Products_CalculationResultsApi_CalcResult_Build/709085:id/dnv_bladed_results-2.1.0b34.tar.gz See example Python script in S:\NetTemp\PDM\ResultsAPI\Rename This shows get_variable and get_group with the new behaviour, using sets of results containing the old and new name. You will need to update root_directory if the script is moved relative to the results folders. Background This change was motivated by a name change to s everal blade variables in 4.17 where the  substring "principal axes" was changed to "principal elastic axes".  E.g. "Blade 1 Mx (Principal axes)" --> "Blade 1 Mx (Principal elastic axes)".  The output group was also renamed "Blade 1 Loads: Principal axes" --> "Blade 1 Loads: Principal elastic axes". Developer documentation See  https://dev.azure.com/dnvdsrenewables/Bladed/_wiki/wikis/Bladed%20Software%20Engineering/2694/Results-API-version-2?anchor=group-and-variable-renaming

112767.title: Prevent external controller to set pitch and torque demand during initialisation
112767.description: dtbladed demands the external controller to set demands for pitch angle/rate and (implicitly) generator torque during the controller initialisation phase.  This has the effect that the recorded results at t=0s do not correspond to the computed steady state values for the signals pitch angle, pitch rate, generator torque and generator air-gap torque.  The expected behaviour should be that the external controller should not be able to set these values during controller initialisation. Acceptance Criteria:# dtbladed should ignore pitch angle/rate and generator torque demands during controller initialisation.

100769.title: [4x][Linearisation] Guidance documentation and improvements
100769.description: Want to improve guidance on what options to include in linearisation calculations. Run through the below calculation options and decide what to auto disable or what to document. Energy systems guidance for linearisation calculations Preparation When running a linearisation, Bladed disables some features automatically. Others need to be disabled manually, as described below. ​Pitch​ actuator The pitch actuator must be linear. Control -> Pitch Actuator -> Input Demand -> Setpoint Trajectory Planning (untick rate and acceleration limits) (disabled - but would like to double check) Control -> Pitch Actuator -> Position Limits - untick all limit switches​​ (disabled) ​Control -> Pitch Actuator -> Actuator Details -> Bearing Friction unticked (disabled) Control -> Pitch Actuator -> Actuator Details -> Actuator Drive Details -> None​ (not disabled) Now adjust the actuator dynamic response to approximate the response due to rate and accel limits that you just removed.  Power train Power Train -> Transmission -> Slipping clutch (off)​ (guidance doc) Power Train -> Losses (Set both to none) (guidance doc) Generator torque limits (are these respected in the linearisation?) Søren : Yes, for VSPR the "Demanded Generator Torque" in the control screen is always used, but for a VSSR, the "Maximum torque demand (Tmax)" is respected. I tested this btw.  Control Control -> Measured Signals -> Turn off all noise (tick) (Are these only applied to the signals received in the external controller?) (Already disabled by Erik, PR 6394, PR 6633) Flexibility​ Linearising with multiple blade parts creates an unmanageable number of modes. You should not be designing a controller so tightly tuned that the differences between linearising with the extra blade parts on or off affects the gains or filters. The new recommendation is to simplify the blade to  single part  and recalculate the modes. (to simplify, just click 'delete selected blade parts' in Flexibility Modeller screen; Bladed will reassign stations automatically. Then re-run modal analysis and check there's no drastic change in modal frequencies) Ensure enough blade modes have damping defined to see blade 1st torsional mode Select 'axial loads only' in Blade geometric stiffness model. https://renewableenergysoftwareportal.dnv.com/KnowledgeBase/Details?productID=1&knowledgeBaseID=223  - some guidance here regarding settings for multi-part blade models ​Aerodynamic control In Bladed 4.8 users are given the option of specifying the aerodynamic model that will be used during linearisation. This allows the user to include the effects of dynamic inflow, dynamic stall and skew wake corrections on the linear model. To get the closest results to legacy aerodynamic models, the user should select ('Specify' menu, 'Aerodynamics control'): Momentum Theory - Axial momentum theory Dynamic wake model - Frozen wake recommended. Mattia said that he'd found that ​Equilibrium wake gave most accurate results, but it sometimes does not work eg causes trouble with unstable blade modes. Skew wake correction model - No skew wake model Simplified airfoil orientation ticked Dynamic stall model - No dynamic stall Future work needs to be done to investigate the costs/benefits of including aerodynamics in the linearisation. However, it has been noted that including aerodynamics can add linear instabilities and make SISO design very slow with a significant increase in the number of model states https://renewableenergysoftwareportal.dnv.com/KnowledgeBase/Details?productID=1&knowledgeBaseID=208  - guidance on best practise settings for the aerodynamics calculation.  ------------------------------------------------------------------ NOTES below regarding observations from users over the years regarding best practise settings for linearisation calculations. *Need refining, this is just notes from a customer. Probably best dealt with through documentation changes* A support query from an external customer (atlantis) has raised a general issue that the default settings in Bladed for the model linearization calculation are not appropriate and not well documented. Make it a feature.  Review this list and then decide what settings should be handled/are handled automtically and document what happens clearly during model linearisation calculation.  To expand on this I have inserted some text from the support query solution and general remarks made by colleagues in the loads aero and control department I’ve changed some settings in the linearisation and it results in a stable model. These are the changes I made. Not all of these will be necessary, so if you’re interested in the details, go through making the changes one by one. A text diff of the $PJ files is sometimes useful. Aerodynamic options: Stall OFF Losses: OFF Linearisation options: Transform rotor modes ON Pitch system options: Setpoint trajectory: Rate limits OFF, Acceleration limits OFF Please also note that in some cases, edgewise blade modes can be slightly unstable. If that’s an issue then add damping in the Modal settings for that mode. Also, it’s possible to have slightly unstable   real   poles. One of these is sometimes “rotor rigid body”. We tend to remove that state from the A, B, C, D matrices. The other is an aerodynamic real pole, which we leave in and stabilise with the controller. Sorry it’s so complex – we know it’s not an ideal situation! To expand on Greg’s point   ‘I don’t understand why Bladed doesn’t just automatically disable these parts when running a model linearization?’,   well I guess me too I’m interested to know if anyone would ever want to run the model linearisation with the following options enabled? If we don’t, then we don’t even need a check box for these, just automatically disable them for linearisations. Control -> Pitch Actuator -> Actuator Details -> Bearing Friction Control -> Pitch Actuator -> Actuator Details -> Actuator Drive Details -> Actuator Torque Limits Control -> Pitch Actuator -> Input Demand -> Setpoint Trajectory Planning (Turn off rate and acceleration limits) Control -> Pitch Actuator -> Position Limits (Turn off all) Control -> Measured Signals -> Turn off all noise (tick) Power Train -> Transmission -> Slipping clutch Power Train -> Losses (Set both to none) However, I agree with your suggestion for the aerodynamic options etc that Marc mentioned. When running Model Linearization calculation there should perhaps be a check box labelled something like ‘Run with standard settings’ that is checked by default and automatically sets all the necessary aerodynamic parameters with the usual settings we use internally.   This could appear alongside a reduced number of carefully selected additional parameters from the existing window (below):   If the user unchecks ‘Run with standard settings’, then all of the other existing options could appear The user should also be able to see a list of what the ‘standard settings’ are to allow them to judge if they are appropriate for whatever they are doing. This could appear in another pop up window, maybe if you hover the mouse over it or something. Erik already removed some of the first order states: Functionality for excluding (ignoring) states for Campbell diagram analysis and model linearization was introduced with commit ID 2f545312 the 24/05/2024 "Merged PR 6394: Linearisation: Ignoring specific 1st order states in Campbell diagram analyses ..." and commit  ID d7b37659 the 07/06/2024 "Merged PR 6633: Linearisation: Ignoring specific 1st order states in Campbell diagram and Model Linearisation analyses" Acceptance Criteria:

Acceptance Criteria The following are the desired behaviours: The Bladed_m72.exe's File Version and Product Version to be adjusted post build so it matches the versioning scheme followed in other components. File version to have the four part format whereas Product Version to have the four part semantic version (e.g. 4.16.0-rc.93.1) The About box then needs adjusting such that it shows the semantic product version (i.e. major.minor.patch, e.g. 4.16.1) The <BladedProject ApplicationVersion=""> within project/$PJ/IN to be populated to match the file version of the m_72.exe. Believe this is automatic but needs testing. The VERSION property within the project/$PJ/IN is also obtained from the m_72.exe's file version until first release and then fixed from then on as a post release activity. So, no special work expected there. Only need to confirm that this property is being set as expected (i.e. with the same four part version as stored in the m_72.exe's file version property)

108326.description: I *believe* that this is actually due to  INIMD not being implemented yet. This tests appears to set a whole load of parameters, then a second set of parameters which should cancel them out.  It makes it very hard to tell exactly *what* is failing.  In this case, it appears to set the nacelle to point to an angle, then rotates the wind to the same angle - effectively neutralising it.  In NG we haven't implemented the initial conditions, so the relative wind is in a very different direction. I would recommend significantly simplifying this test, so that if it fails it is possible to determine *why*.

108696.description: Requirement to add a row to the blade GUI screen to enable users to enter " Principal shear axes orientation " and connect that to the .IN, .prj and .$PJ file via the key BETA_PS contained in BSTIFFMB. Add "Use default principal shear axes orientation", or " Use default shear axes orientation " if too long, field that defaults to the "Principal axis orientation"   (NB! This is different than the default behavior for the other two orientations and is intended). It should only be available when the shear stiffness is switched on, and otherwise grayed out similar to what is done for "Use default principal axis orientation" when "Stiffness" is switched off.  Upgrade behavior should be that " Use default principal shear axis orientation " is switched on.  Note that Shear should not be capitalized as is shown in the figure, but be lowercase " Principal shear axes orientation " and " Use default shear axes orientation ".  Hover-over text for "Principal shear axes orientation" should be the following: "Orientation of principal shear axes (xs, ys): defaults to principal structural axes of stiffness (xp, yp)" Optional specifications depending on the difficulty: ( Look at attached work doc instead ) Current hover-over text for "Principal axis orientation" should be the following: " Orientation of principal elastic axes (xp, yp): defaults to aerodynamic twist axes " "Principal axis orientation" should be "Principal elastic axes orientation" "Mass axis orientation" should be "Principal inertia axes orientation" "Stiffness" option should be "Bending stiffness" "Use default principal axis orientation" should be "Use default principal elastic axes orientation" "Axial Degree of Freedom" should be "Axial stiffness" "Torsional degree of freedom" should be "Torsional stiffness" ======================================================================= Additional details - (CK): Expected Change In ".prj/$pj/.in" Files: Two new variables expected. BETA_PS => represents the “Principal shear axes orientation” variable and its values. DEF_BETA_PS => represents the "Use default shear axes orientation" checkbox and its state. (0= false, -1 = true) Example: Upon checking 'Shear stiffness', these are the variables that should now be populated. Blade Graph Window: Ensure the graph for  “Principal shear axes orientation” variable  get displayed.  Ensure the title & axes is correct Ensure the graph matches with the table values. Blade Cross Section: In the blade cross section part on the blade window, change  "Neutral axis" to "Elastic centre". Acceptance Criteria: Reading the inputs (.in file has correct format and they are read correctly by dtbladed) UI modified Upgrade / downgrade behaviour implemented encryption default behaviour working with the new feature GH reporter file reflects new field Update Project API process Requirements have been successfully met & manual and automated testing passed

109879.title: [2OHWarnings] Improve issuing warnings on second order hydro QTF data

This might indicate that there is redundant or even conflicting input data for some QTFs It is recommended to use only input QTFs for 0 <= direction < 360 deg., dtbladed will account for the periodicity. Input values outside that interval have been wrapped to values inside the interva l.   This warning was issued 2 times (probably once for Diff QTF and one for Sum QTF). 2. *** WARNING:   DIFF QTF - Note the input QTF data has a directional discretization larger than 30 deg. ​ However, this warning was issued only for Diff QTF. Is Bladed using a cylindrical coordinate system if you have ticked ‘Symmetric in X and Y’?   Thank for looking into the other items!   Med vennlig hilsen / Best regards Vetle Kallåk Specialist, Hydrodynamics Wind Energy and Renewables, Norway COWI COWI AS

110476.title: [4x][Linearisation][UI] Warn user that MBC option is switched off when upgrading
110476.description: Context. When a user upgrades an earlier model that meets the following criteria (see below) into 4.17,  they should receive a warning.  Criteria: Calculation number should be Model Linearisation or Campbell Diagram (CALCN == 17 OR CALCN == 16) and Multi-blade coordinate (MBC) transform option is switched off (TransformRotorModesToNonRotating == false) and if the model is a Campbell Diagram then The mode of operation should be power production ( ModeOfOperation is "ModeOfOperation_PowerProduction") Warning issued should be: " Current project has the multi-blade coordinate (MBC) transform option off. It is recommended to enable this option. For further details on this option, please consult the web-based documentation in the Theory Manual under Model Linearisation, Multi-Blade Coordinate Transformation for Linearised Models. " Acceptance Criteria: Warning is issued as expected based on the criteria above and not elsewise

110529.title: Animation: Members appear misaligned when rotated using node structural orientation
110529.description: When tower members are rotated at each output timestep using their structural orientation value of the node at end 1, they appear to be slightly misaligned, e.g.:  This is because the members are in fact bending (as clarified with Will), and a more accurate visual representation would be to plot them as splines instead of using a tube filter with straight sides (suggested as a future item). An interim workaround is to stop applying the node orientation and instead calculate the member rotation required to plot the members exactly between nodes at each step.  As the node positions at each timestep is known in advance, the rotations can be pre-calculated to reduce overhead during playback.

Regardless of whether pitch actuator is encrypted or not.  - Pass When user clicks 'Ok' on  Pitch Actuator screen  a warning message should come up. - Pass User should be made aware that  "Pitch drive demand perturbation" cannot be selected if "Response to Rate demand" in the Pitch Actuator screen in not set to "Closed Loop (PID)". - Pass If user switches from "Closed Loop (PID)" to another response type and   "Pitch drive demand perturbation" is selected  then model linearisation cannot be run.

111105.description: We want to only import the data fields filled in the converted tools ( sifconverter, wamit , aqwa) . This allows : to use wdnh produced in versions earlier then 4.17 in Bladed 4.17 [ not compatible at the moment ] fix the following problem in the wdnh import function :  if the user changes some of the BEM hydro settings in the UI , and then imports wdnh data  - these choices will be overwritten by the import ( using data model default values).  So the user can be using other settings than the ones he originally set. This can be dangerous , as the user may not be aware of this.  Just reading the required fields from the wdnh file will remove this problem , as only data read from the flow solver data will be used.  Acceptance Criteria: Given the complexity of this work, we decided to improve just the warning message. 107700.title: [4.16] Implement 'Promotion Release' build
107700.description: There is currently no 'Promotion Release' build implemented on 'main' and hence any release branches taken from 'main' post Git-migration. A new 'promotion release' build should be implemented on 'main', and cherry-picked to '4.16'. This will be very similar in behaviour to the existing promotion builds on the historic release branches. Acceptance Criteria Ensures win and linux installers are produced with the release version numbers (now 'major'.'minor'.'patch') Build approval is required from 'release approvers' group The promotion build is only provisioned for a release branch deployment, not for 'main' or 'dev' branches Publishes all artifacts to network location for releases, so they aren't cleaned up accidently Documentation is updated: Release Promotion (Git): 4.16 and above   ( Web view ) Implementation Add a new context parameter 'bladed.deployment.mode' = 'release' | 'dev' = 'dev' to control whether to create the promotion build update the 'release branch' wiki page to include this, update the 'Release / TemplateRoot / Main / ContextParameters' to include this Copy the structure of the release promotion build from 4.15 vcs root: checkout rules = "+:Build" this build doesn't actually need any source files, but it needs to have a VCS root, to restrict to default branch, and to apply the git tag, so 'Build' is a smallish folder to use here showDependenciesChanges = true branch filter set to '<default>' Step 2: Check consistency of Linux and Windows builds Modify to ensure the major-minor-patch versions of both Windows and Linux match %Windows Installer.Version.MajorMinorPatch% ==  %DtbladedLinux.Version.MajorMinorPatch% Parameters: Promote.Target.FileVersion rename to  Promote.Target.Version =  %Windows Installer.Version.MajorMinorPatch% Promote.Target.DirectoryPath = resolveNetworkReleaseRootPath(%Promote.Target.Version%) Build approval: approvedBuild(ExternalRefs.BLADED_RELEASE_APPROVERS_GROUP_ID) Git tagging: gitTaggingBuild(DslContext.settingsRoot, "bladed/ %Promote.Target.Version% ")

As there is a very soft mooring and a strong current at 90deg, the floater drifts hundreds of metres in the Y direction. This should (?) cause it to move out of the wind grid and thus you'd expect to see the wind drop to zero. But that is not what happens: There is a support case 00182906 associated with this.

107364.title: [FOWTresonat] Fix the bug on Main
107364.description: Investigation showed that hydrostatic stiffness is responsible for this resonant-like of motion when a load is applied from the side of the turbine.  Hydrostatic stiffness uses RotVec(a_wHydroSystemKinematics.GetBodyOrientation(this->m_sName)).vec()    to find the vector of  orientational components (Roll, Pitch, and Yaw) in the current implementation. It seems this function can not calculate orientation properly specifically when a single BEM body is simulated. FIX: It should change to use extrinsic representation of bryant angle using   bryant_angles().

105906.title: [4X][Ext loads DLL] Update the documentation
105906.description: Original code comments: /// <summary>A function to set friction to a multibody add-on component</summary> /// <param name=component_id>The name of the multibody add-on component, as it appears in the documentation.</param> /// <param name=friction>The friction to be set on add-on component</param> virtual void SetKineticFriction(const std::string& component_id, const double& friction) = 0; /// <summary>A function to set additional kinetic friction to a multibody add-on component</summary> /// <param name=component_id>The name of the multibody add-on component, as it appears in the documentation.</param> /// <param name=addfriction>The additional kinetic friction to be set on add-on component</param> virtual void AddKineticFriction(const std::string& component_id, const double& addfriction) = 0; Revised: /// <summary>A function to set the kinetic friction to a multibody add-on component. This will override any previously set friction by Bladed or within the external loads DLL.  /// The value of kinetic friction set by this call remains constant until modified by either Bladed or by the external loads DLL. /// However, it is recommended to set the friction every iteration.</summary> /// <param name=component_id>The name of the multibody add-on component, as it appears in the documentation.</param> /// <param name=friction>The friction to be set on add-on component</param> virtual void SetKineticFriction(const std::string& component_id, const double& friction) = 0; /// <summary>A function to add additional kinetic friction to a multibody add-on component. This will increase the amount of friction /// that is already set by Bladed or the external loads DLL. The additional kinetic friction is reset to zero by Bladed at the start of each  ApplyExternalLoads call and the user must set this value each time if desired. /// Multiple AddKineticFriction function calls during the same calculation call sequence are not recommended as only the last function call will be applied. </summary> /// <param name=component_id>The name of the multibody add-on component, as it appears in the documentation.</param> /// <param name=addfriction>The additional kinetic friction to be set on add-on component</param> virtual void AddKineticFriction(const std::string& component_id, const double& addfriction) = 0;
